programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates Louise's cat Pip, a one-eyed cerebral hypoplasia feline, which will not stop tormenting and attacking the owner's other cat, Red. Plus, Erin's moggy Autumn causes havoc by urinating in several places around the home, including the settee, beds, kitchen worktops, microwave, computer monitor and backpacks

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps expectant parents Sara and John with their aggressive cat, Lucy. Plus, Keri wants to become an animal psychiatrist, but the daily fights between her two felines make her doubt her chosen career path

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Wardens Reneau and Stovall suspect a couple of illegally shooting a deer. Later, Warden Iles transports orphaned armadillo pups to be rehabilitated

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff and his team help a dog that was lost during a windstorm. Dr Baier treats a small bird with a big personality and Susan rescues an abandoned paralysed dog

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson is called upon to design a bear observation treehouse for two sisters on their 1,000-acre property and creates his first-ever mid-century decagon house with glass walls

programme.title: Rugged Justice
programme.desc: Officers race to rescue passengers on a capsized boat and arrest a suspect with multiple drug warrants. A bear carcass is put to good use by a local tribe

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier

programme.desc: After discovering a dangerous alpha bigfoot leading a tree clan, the team designs a trap including a bed of nails to capture the aggressive leader

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys must move an entire cabin into the flood zone as Marty's dam is put to the test. The surprise arrival of chicks means the coop needs an upgrade

programme.title: Blonde vs Bear
programme.desc: Documentary following Ann Bryant as she tries to protect bears from tourists in Lake Tahoe. As the summer continues more bears are breaking into houses and Ann battles to save them from certain death, but is it too late for this mother and her cubs?

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson takes a tour of the USA to visit a few spectacular treehouses in the company of fellow enthusiasts and builders

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: It is feeding time at the sanctuary, and the primate inhabitants must learn the tricky task of finding food for themselves

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: The guys are asked to build a Harry Houdini inspired tank for magicians in Los Angeles. Plus, Wayde King plans out how to celebrate his wedding anniversary to Heather

programme.desc: A cruelty investigator is responding to a call from the sheriff's department. A pony has been dragged down a road, leaving it crippled with pain

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys must move an entire cabin into the flood zone as Marty's dam is put to the test. The surprise arrival of chicks means the coop needs an upgrade

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.sub-title: The Dogs Who Stole Our Hearts
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the pit bull shelter, beginning with a look at the dogs that had a major impact on Tia's family and the parolees who helped them

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Kristen, whose cat Moose makes a misery of her life by marking everything in her house. The expert works his techniques to transform its ways

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a woman whose cat has not used a litter box for two years, and another troubled feline terrorises a fellow cat and its owner

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Game Wardens Gould and Hickerson chase drug smugglers that crossed the Rio Grande River. Meanwhile, Game Warden Boone checks out an injured kingfisher bird

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Mel and her daughter rescue a baby squirrel. Dr Jeff helps a dog with a serious illness, and then teams up with Dr Petra to save a beloved cat hit by a car

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his team build a butterfly-themed treehouse in a cluster of six trees, featuring a climbing net and a loft, for the residents of a therapeutic centre

programme.title: Gator Boys
programme.desc: Paul and Jimmy find themselves busy capturing alligators on a school playground and in a housing development. Meanwhile, Tre is bitten by a giant Burmese python

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Always pushed to their limits by the elements, circumstances and each other, the Kilchers continue to work together as a family to sustain the rich heritage of homesteading

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: Following four groups of ordinary people as they travel into the wilderness of Alaska, where they must find a new home in the inhospitable environment and deal with the threat posed by bears, glaciers and their own fears

programme.title: Alaska Monsters
programme.sub-title: Bigfoot of Cook Inlet: Water Stalker
programme.desc: The native outdoorsmen go after a water-based bigfoot, meaning they must use speedboats, kayaks and water traps in an attempt to capture this elusive creature

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys create their first-ever self-automated homestead in the fierce Alaskan wilderness, while firefighters Kim and Casey Boespflug are away on duty

programme.title: Dog Detectives
programme.desc: Devastating news about PC McIntyre's Alsatian Rudi jeopardises their future together. Meanwhile, sniffer dog Meg has the chance to show her skills on a suspected drug dealer's car

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson shares some of his favourite Treehouse Masters moments, including some previously lost footage

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Investigator Max Mixson visits a house where more than 100 dogs are reportedly being confined in miserable conditions, but is given an angry reception by the owner

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Investigator Scott Wernick is unable to save the life of an emaciated horse, but later witnesses the birth of a calf

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps a soft-spoken musician who feels abused by her cat and is ashamed she cannot figure out how to prevent her pet's behaviour

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: Lake Windsor on Inagua Island is home to a mystery. How did giant ocean-dwelling fish get there? The team aims to find out - and catch some bonefish

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Learn to Love or Love to Learn
programme.desc: Las Vegas Weddings asks tank manufacturing supremos Brett Raymer and Wayde King to make an old-school Vegas-style tank for their chapel

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Animal control officers have been removing cats from an apartment where a woman has recently been evicted. Some of the animals are in bad shape and need help urgently

programme.title: Florence Nightingale: Nursing Pioneer
programme.desc: A profile of the woman who revolutionised modern nursing and reformed healthcare at home and abroad. The documentary follows her life, from being born into a rich English family, and revealing how she broke through social and gender barriers to pioneer a profession previously reserved for men. Dedicating her life to helping those in need, she was a trailblazer who led a group of nurses to care for wounded soldiers during the Crimean War and developed revolutionary views about hygiene and sanitation, going on to establish a training school and reform healthcare at home and abroad

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: This edition takes a look at when Fabio Capello led Milan to a new era of glory, while Berlusconi stepped onto Italy's political stage, ushering in a turbulent chapter in the nation's politics

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.desc: Profile of the ancient Hittite civilisation, looking at what archaeologists found when they uncovered its lost capital, Hattusha in modern-day Turkey, including temples, palaces and a pyramid-like structure facing Egypt. The historians also discovered a library charting the rise and fall of the empire, which vanished 3,000 years ago. Narrated by Mark Halliley

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams from Gonville and Caius College, Cambridge, and the University of St Andrews put fingers on buzzers as round one of the quiz competition for students continues. Jeremy Paxman hosts

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents a quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. This episode, the Bankers face the Bookkeepers

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Helen Czerski explores the science of temperature. She begins by venturing to the bottom of the scale, revealing how cold has shaped the world and why frozen does not necessarily mean what people might think

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter turns her attention to Ruth's trial, which took just a day and a half. She starts with a tape-recorded conversation from the 1980s between Ruth's son Andre and the barrister who led the prosecution. Andre expresses doubts about his mother's trial, calling into question her state of mind and whether she was a cold-blooded killer

programme.title: Roy Clarke Remembers - A Foreign Field
programme.desc: Writer Roy Clarke tells the story of how his one-off drama A Foreign Field came to life. He also speaks about the role that Sir Alec Guinness played in getting the drama off the ground and his feelings on seeing one of his scripts being performed by a cast of film icons. The drama follows the story of war veterans returning to the beaches of France

programme.title: Screen One
programme.desc: Two British Second World War veterans travel to Normandy to visit the grave of a deceased friend and search for a French woman who brought joy into their lives. On their travels, they encounter a former American soldier who is also in the region to reminisce about his wartime days, but is being hindered by his bickering son and daughter-in-law. Comedy drama from 1993, with Alec Guinness and Leo McKern

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the flamboyant court of French monarch Louis XIV at the Palace of Versailles, where he surrounded himself with aristocrats, artists, interior designers, gardeners, wig-makers, chefs and musicians. Ambitious courtiers scrambled to get closer to the king, but the bedchamber seemed to provide a more unseemly path to power

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the flamboyant court of French monarch Louis XIV at the Palace of Versailles, where he surrounded himself with aristocrats, artists, interior designers, gardeners, wig-makers, chefs and musicians. Ambitious courtiers scrambled to get closer to the king, but the bedchamber seemed to provide a more unseemly path to power

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Helen Czerski explores the science of temperature. She begins by venturing to the bottom of the scale, revealing how cold has shaped the world and why frozen does not necessarily mean what people might think

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter turns her attention to Ruth's trial, which took just a day and a half. She starts with a tape-recorded conversation from the 1980s between Ruth's son Andre and the barrister who led the prosecution. Andre expresses doubts about his mother's trial, calling into question her state of mind and whether she was a cold-blooded killer

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman presents the long-running academic quiz show, in which teams from the University of Sheffield and University College London compete in another first round match

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: The Cricket Supporters take on IT Support in the quiz where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Hosted by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: The Morecambe and Wise Show
programme.sub-title: The Morecambe and Wise Show 1968: The Lost Tape
programme.desc: Comedy duo Eric Morecambe and Ernie Wise go a few rounds in the boxing ring and pay a visit to a nudist colony. With music by female singers the Paper Dolls and Greek guitar group Trio Athénée. First broadcast September 16, 1968

programme.title: The Perfect Morecambe and Wise
programme.desc: Another selection of classic sketches and routines from the double act's shows. Eric dons a wig, Tom Jones performs Exactly Like You with the aid of some unlikely backing singers and Judi Dench joins the duo in a parody of the story of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. Plus, the boys get musical in a performance of There Is Nothing Like a Dame from South Pacific

programme.title: Dame Judi Dench - Five to Eleven
programme.desc: Judi Dench reads The Cultivation of Christmas Trees by TS Eliot and The Cherry Tree Carol Poem by Anon. First broadcast 20 December 1988

programme.title: The Miracle Club (2023)
programme.desc: Chrissie Ahearn arrives in the town of Ballygar from Boston for the funeral of her mother Maureen, 40 years after she was banished by friends and family as a pregnant teenager. Her return coincides with a pilgrimage to Lourdes organised by Father Dermot Byrne. Comedy set in 1968, starring Maggie Smith, Kathy Bates and Laura Linney

programme.title: Personal Cinema: Maggie Smith
programme.desc: In a programme first broadcast in 1970, the actress talks to Michael Aspel about her career and chooses scenes from some of her favourite films, including Way Out West, The Last Hurrah, On the Waterfront, Some Like It Hot and The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: Reggae at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of reggae performances from the BBC archives, featuring footage from programmes including Top of the Pops, Old Grey Whistle Test and Later with Jools Holland. Acts include Bob Marley and the Wailers, Gregory Isaacs, Desmond Dekker, Burning Spear, Althea and Donna, Dennis Brown and Buju Banton

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the North European region through its art. He begins by examining the links between landscape, climate and national character, taking in everything from the era of the Vikings to Edvard Munch's The Scream

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: State Opening of Parliament 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage from the Palace of Westminster as King Charles outlines the government's plans for the coming year in what has become a unique combination of pageantry and politics to mark the formal start of the new parliamentary session

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Carmarthenshire where he learns about land speed records at Pendine Sands

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Ethical hacker Jim Browning joins Rav Wilding and Nick Stapleton in the hub as the team keep an eye on a scam group operating out of Albania. They claim to be able to help their victims recover lost cryptocurrency investments - but in reality they are out to steal more money

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton faces his toughest assignment to date, as he tries to find broadcasting icon and one of TV's most opinionated panellists, Janet Street-Porter, a Suffolk sanctuary - away from the many daily irritations she'd happily banish from existence. Despite Janet's refreshingly short and sweet wishlist, it proves to be a tricky challenge satisfying this exacting house hunter, but Alistair has an ace up his sleeve, which sees the property search take an epic detour to a remote Hebridean island

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse helps two Eurovision enthusiasts and their cat to find a new home in the Cumbrian countryside, and meets a young family of keen climbers and outdoor adventurers

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk travel to Swindon where a local woodworking group need help repairing an aeroplane-shaped bench that pays tribute to the town's aviation history. Dom meets Tulsi, who brings with her a Hindu holy scripture passed down from her grandmother that's so fragile it can't be opened without further damage being caused and Will learns how to make skeps

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Abi, Gary, Jo and Thom aim to win big by stepping in the right direction, avoiding the wrong answers and making it across the bridge to the other side. Game show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Vernon Kay host the magazine show, with celebrity guests and stories of interest from around the UK

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant makes a situation worse, Bea panics about her actions, and Nicola realises she is out of options

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the race shifts up a gear as the four remaining teams enter Mongolia, their eighth and final country. Facing freezing temperatures and rugged off-road travel, the intrepid duos set out on an expedition across the endless steppe and deep into the unknown, traversing landscapes few travellers have ever experienced. It tests every ounce of their resilience as they battle to take pole position at the final checkpoint before the finish line

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda is thrilled when a hipster coffee shop opens in Soha and offers to promote the cafe on Senous. Meanwhile, Anne unexpectedly finds fame after a video she posted goes viral. Comedy, starring Lucy Punch and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: A depressed Richard searches for a real job and embarks on a fitness journey as his 40th birthday looms large, while Ken prepares for his first 'first' date in almost 60 years. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: The Spin (2025)
programme.desc: Two music-loving friends have two days to travel the length of Ireland to save their record store from closure. To make things more difficult, they don't have a car. But it soon turns out that's the least of their problems. Comedy, starring Tara Lynne O'Neill, Brenock O'Connor and Owen Colgan

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Bank Devalued Our House After Approving Our Mortgage
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Louise Minchin and Julia Somerville investigate why a bank suddenly refused to let one couple re-mortgage because of the way their home had been constructed - despite approving the mortgage five years earlier. Plus, a woman being hounded for payments for a phone contract that isn't hers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is at a mid-terrace house in Stoke-on-Trent that seems too good to be true and Jacqui Joseph is fretting over the short lease on a flat in south-east London. A terraced house in Staffordshire needs a lot more attention than originally thought

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams search for money-making items in Llandeilo, Carmarthenshire, with the help of Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson. Presenter Christina Trevanion gets hands-on with the ancient art of silversmithing at a family business in the nearby town of Laugharne

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Nick Stapleton listen in to calls by a scammer posing as a representative of Sky's broadband department as he tries to steal money from UK victims believing he is fixing their internet connection. But as the team try to contact a woman in the process of being defrauded, they face a previously unseen foe - a fire alarm in the scam hub

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Television and radio presenter Angellica Bell and her husband, former fellow children's TV favourite Michael Underwood, team up with Nicki Chapman to find a family home in the Devon countryside. The property savvy pair show they're still happy to roll up their sleeves and get mucky, as they take on a DIY miniature dream home build, and there are even talks of full-scale renovations at a 1930s period proposal

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having been to the Lake District over 50 times, today's couple have decided to stop visiting and start living in one of the UK's most spectacular national parks. The about-to-be-retired couple need Steve Brown's help to find them, and their two rescue cats, a new forever home in the Cumbrian countryside

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.sub-title: Glasgow - A Fix at the Top of the Cathedral
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Lucia Scalisi help repair Glasgow Cathedral's weathervane at the top of the 225-foot-high spire, save a Chinese unicorn mask made of bamboo and paper that plays and important part in celebrations for Glasgow's Hakka Chinese Community, and discover how street art is changing the city

programme.title: Bridge of Lies

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea is out of options, Mark and Grant's relationship is more strained than ever, while Cindy tries to get through to George

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: The second semi-final live from Vienna, Austria, which sees another 15 acts taking the stage in the hope of winning one of the 10 places in Saturday's main show. UK viewers can vote for their favourites, and there's also a chance to see and hear UK act Look Mum No Computer, aka YouTube singer Sam Battle, as he takes to the stage for his first live performance of Eins, Zwei, Drei, which he will be hoping will bag him more than nul points on Saturday

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents topical debate from Brecon, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate how a scammer was able to cancel one man's flight when he believed he was receiving customer support from the airline on social media. Plus, the woman whose cottage holiday didn't live up to expectations - but didn't receive a penny in compensation

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: The glamour model and kidnapping victim recalls her ordeal, orchestrated by Black Death. Once safely under the protection of the Italian authorities, she had to give evidence in court against the man who enabled her escape from the international sex-trafficking gang - Lukasz Herba. Chloe then returned home to the UK, to find herself plunged into ordeal as the Italian courts publicly released details of the case and the ongoing investigation to the world's press. What she hoped to bury as an embarrassing nightmare soon ignited the interest of the global media

programme.title: The Instagram Effect
programme.desc: The story of the social media giant and how it became the dominant force it is today, told by former staff and insiders as well as through powerful testimony from users who have been negatively impacted. The film unpacks in intricate detail how Instagram's architecture and algorithms have the power to affect human behaviour, and reveals the important decisions that led Instagram to become the company it is

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Documentary following boxer Tommy Fury's return to the ring. Tommy is at his lowest point personally and professionally, living alone in a house that was meant to be his and partner Molly-Mae's 'forever home'. His role as a father to baby Bambi brings both joy and pressure as he tries to convince Molly-Mae he is a changed man. After finally getting the medical all-clear to fight again, Tommy and his trainer and father John prepare for his return to the ring against MMA fighter Darren Till. Team Fury travels to the press conference - with John promising to be on his best behaviour

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy prepares for his first gym appearance since announcing his comeback, but with fighter Darren Till throwing threats and chaos into the mix, trainer John pulls Tommy out of the fight claiming he is unwilling to risk his son's future career. It's a crushing blow for Tommy, who's desperate for momentum and purpose. Amid the pressure, there's a flicker of hope - Tommy and partner Molly-Mae Hague are spotted together on New Year's Eve, with a kiss that hints at a brighter future

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall dives with 100 grey nurse sharks and joins a mission in Indonesia to save sharks from over-fishing with eco-tourism

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve is underwater in Indonesia with funny-looking frogfish, a stargazer, flamboyant cuttlefish, a mimic octopus and a nightmare of nature - the bobbit worm

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The team travel to the Royal Navy Air Station in Yeovilton, Somerset, where they are recruited to transform the communal spaces - officially known as mess decks - used by the new recruits for socialising, eating and relaxing. The six designers are given a room each and Michelle Ogundehin's brief is to create spaces that are practical and comfortable, to provide an escape from the regimented daily routine of life on the air station - and it isn't long before the beige walls and boring tables are being replaced by something much more colourful. What will Michelle and guest judge Kelly Holmes - who spent 10 years in the military herself - make of the changes?

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: The model shares the story of her 2017 abduction and tries to come to terms with what took place, the media reaction and why many people refused to believe her. Alongside Chloe's unfolding account of what happened, the series features archive footage and testimony from her former agent, detectives from the Italian and British police, the Italian legal team and the judge in the kidnappers' trial

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: Chloe reveals a turning point in her captivity, when she realised she might be able to persuade one of the gang to help her, and recalls leaving the farmhouse with her captor, uncertain if she was being handed over to the authorities or sold into sex slavery

programme.title: The Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: The work of a team of LGBTQ+ professional party agents as they plan epic events. Best friends Jordan and Tre from London visit the agency. They want to throw an amazing birthday bash for Jordan's boyfriend Corey, but that's only part of the brief - Jordan also wants to spring a surprise proposal on Corey and he's hoping the Big Proud Party Agency can help him with the most important moment of his life

programme.title: The Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: The agents are tasked with creating an epic night out for a group of friends soon to head to university

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: One-off comedy about a group of teenagers with varying disabilities on their ride to school on the mobility bus. Mike thinks he is better than his group of 'loser' friends, but it's difficult to break away from them when they all get the bus together. Starring Jack Carroll

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: How animals survive when the northern half of Asia is transformed by snow and ice each year. Featuring footage of Himalayan wolves hunting the fast-moving chiru antelope, red-crowned cranes learning to perform intricate mating dances, and Amur tigers in the forests where Russia, China, and North Korea meet - an area that is also home to the world's largest owls

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff's Field of Dreams
programme.desc: As they reach the halfway point on the tour, some of the group start to struggle and the success of the trip is thrown into question. Freddie prepares the boys for their toughest cricket match so far and smarten them up for a prestigious event with members of Kolkata's high society

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: A face-to-face mission brings out the players' true colours, leading to arguments and new suspicions. At the Circle of Shaq, new players face accusations and the game takes an unexpected turn when the traitors are handed out an explosive secret mission

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: The morning begins with a chilling announcement that leaves the players stunned - someone's fate hangs in the balance after the events of the previous night. As the group retraces their steps, whispers of suspicion and fear ripple through - and the mission takes on an ominous tone as the players work together to uncover who has been murdered

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff's Field of Dreams
programme.desc: As they reach the halfway point on the tour, some of the group start to struggle and the success of the trip is thrown into question. Freddie prepares the boys for their toughest cricket match so far and smarten them up for a prestigious event with members of Kolkata's high society

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: The disappearance of 17-year-old Andie Bell has haunted the town of Little Kilton for five years, a confessional text pointed the blame at her boyfriend Sal after he took his own life. Now, inquisitive teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi is determined to clear his name and get to the bottom of what really happened. Crime drama based on the novel by Holly Jackson, starring Emma Myers and Zain Iqbal

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Now a team, Pip and Ravi turn their attention to Andie's best friends and find out Andie had a secret life that Sal was unhappy about. Meanwhile, Pip receives an anonymous warning. Crime drama, starring Emma Myers and Zain Iqbal

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Against her better judgement, Pip seeks out a calamity party with hopes of tracking down the drug dealer Max told her about. Ravi is sceptical about the plan but she's determined to see it through with the help of Cara and Lauren

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: A look at how, in 1980, a booby-trapped bomb accompanied by a $3 million ransom note terrorised a casino in Lake Tahoe, Nevada. As the FBI raced against the clock, each step uncovered dark secrets and unexpected motives. This programme follows the FBI's investigations into the identity of the calculating mind behind this sophisticated device and elaborate extortion plan

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Exploring how technology is transforming life for deaf people, from communication tools and AI to new ways of connecting, working and living in a digital world. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined in the studio by Billy-Jack Gerrard. He explains how AI works, how coding underpins everyday technology and how his own experiences as a deaf coder have influenced his AI career path and thinking

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: A look at how, in 1980, a booby-trapped bomb accompanied by a $3 million ransom note terrorised a casino in Lake Tahoe, Nevada. As the FBI raced against the clock, each step uncovered dark secrets and unexpected motives. This programme follows the FBI's investigations into the identity of the calculating mind behind this sophisticated device and elaborate extortion plan

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: At the Western General Hospital, 48-year-old Alastair has a rare penis cancer that requires a partial amputation. Consultant gynaecologist Cameron Martin treats Laura from Edinburgh, who has stage 4 endometriosis. She has a huge cyst and a damaged kidney that both need to be removed

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper visits Railway Wood, a replica First World War trench in Kent based on one at the Battle of Passchendaele in Belgium. Meanwhile, two teams of antiques hunters go shopping for collectibles with the help of Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway tour through Uzbekistan on Tashkent's suburban electric railway to one of the most secretive pieces of Soviet Cold War engineering. From Tashkent, he picks up the high-speed train west to Samarkand and discovers how China's Belt and Road Initiative is re-establishing old Silk Road routes with new railway connections

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Japan for a trip through the country, beginning his first leg in its second city Osaka, which is famous for its food culture, nightlife and friendly people. He then takes a ride on a Shinkansen bullet train and end in Nagoya, Japan's manufacturing hub

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Travelling by rail across South Korea, Michael Portillo explores one of the world's most successful modern nations, shaped by division, resilience and rapid change. He begins his journey at the demilitarised zone, the heavily fortified border that has divided the Korean peninsula since the end of the Korean War, then travels south by rail into the country's capital Seoul, which has become one of the world's most advanced cities

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Helen and Simon Holroyd sample life in Australia for a week to find out if a move there might help save their relationship, which is under strain from Simon's heavy work schedule. Nicki Chapman presents

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A fashion student renovates a two-bedroom cottage with a major crack in it in New Malden, London while more seasoned developers in Leeds and Manchester tackle issues in their terraces in hopes of turning a profit on their online auction buys

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, who answer question on the specialist subjects of Emily Wilding Davison, The Thick of It, the New Romantics, and 20th-century US vice-presidents, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Retired couple Steven and Sue love their two-bedroom bungalow, but Steven's mobility issues have left Sue taking on more of the workload, and their tired, cluttered shed does not help matters. The team sets out to turn the shed into a practical and welcoming space for Sue to do her volunteer work, and make it easier for Steven to access his mobility scooter

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Brad Bestelink witnesses Xudum the leopard fight to save her newborn cubs, Anna Dimitriadis finds a brave cheetah thriving in the heart of lion territory, and Vianet Djenguet tracks lioness Tsebe as she risks her life to feed her five cubs. As the lion pride continues to grow, becoming the largest in Africa, competition for food leads to chaos and infighting. Could this pride's success also be it downfall?

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team visits Whitchurch in Shropshire to examine valuable items, including a pair of silver claret jugs, Irish wooden peat buckets made in the early 1800s and a collection of card cases, snuff boxes and vinaigrettes

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Some of the best unseen finds from the show's valuation days from around the country, including visits to the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke and Ragley Hall in Warwickshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure in Uzbekistan continues in Samarkand, one of the oldest cities in Central Asia. He discovers the national passion for bread, leading him to a family bakery where he learns how to bake special, bagel-like loaves. The great conqueror, Tamerlane, made Samarkand his capital in the 14th-century, and at his tomb Michael learns of the brutality and military prowess of the man who is today Uzbekistan's national hero

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williamsheads up the Tywi river valley. His 75-mile journey begins at the coastal village of Llansteffan, where the river meets the sea under the watchful gaze of its 12th-century Norman castle. Following the river inland through the fertile lowlands, Iolo visits Bishop's Pond, by Abergwili. At Dinefwr Park near Llandeilo, Iolo heads into the parkland during the autumn for the Fallow Deer rutting season

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah travels from California to Japan to experience the latest age-defying techniques and examine the true meaning of life

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah Fry uncovers and literally un-packs the secrets behind the technologies of the modern world in a series of short clips

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: As Elon Musk's businesses reach unprecedented levels of success, his public profile grows - but his behaviour comes under ever greater scrutiny. The episode starts in 2016, when a new president came to power, whose support Elon would need in order to fulfill his multi-billion dollar government contracts. His marriage to wife Talulah Riley comes to an end and they divorce for a second time, and as well as a fractured private life, the pressure on Elon across his empire becomes enormous, with cracks beginning to show at Tesla, amid the threat of bankruptcy and whistleblowers going to the press

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Livestock farmer Joyce has a rare tumour in her bile duct and is undergoing a complex operation to have it removed. During the life-saving procedure, surgeons Anya Adair and Professor Steve Wigmore encounter a difficult dilemma with huge consequences. Also, surgeon Malcolm Will has taken on a rare case involving a tumour that has invaded his patient's chest wall

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: On patrol in Belfast, Perri and Adam respond to reports of cars performing dangerous stunts on the streets while large crowds look on, but the cars scatter when they arrive and the officers don't catch anyone. The team also receives reports of two women being disruptive at a hotel

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: With the start of the tournament weeks away, final plans for the opening ceremony need to be pinned down. But its Mexican creative director has had a rethink, and his new vision doesn't include any reference to America at all, or indeed to soccer. The lead events producer may or may not be close to a having a breakdown. Also, Ian finds himself starting to think about life after football

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBC's world affairs editor reflects on the week's major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: A look at how, meticulous and methodical, Big John engineered his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it was flawless, but as the deadline approached, nothing went according to plan. Two battles unfolded simultaneously - a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six more home cooks put themselves in the culinary spotlight in the second heat of the week, ready to show judges Anna Haugh and Grace Dent that they have what it takes. The Signature Dish round sees global influences in abundance, with Polish, Mexican and Brazilian flavours making for an exciting tasting, while Grace and Anna set a daunting Classic Recipe test, which requires the cooks to follow a recipe for an egg yolk and ricotta-filled raviolo, served with a sage and mushroom burnt butter

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Guest judge Patrick Grant joins Diarmuid and Carol as they explore three more contenders for Northern Ireland's Greatest Garden. They visit a coastal sanctuary in Millisle, a 15-acre woodland and wildlife garden in Ballymoney, and a country cottage with herbaceous borders and a cut flower garden in Greyabbey

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Heat three gets underway, which means six new amateur cooks have left their home kitchens ready to compete for one of four coveted MasterChef aprons. The Signature Dish round features a meal they would serve to impress friends or family, before the next challenge requires them to make an authentic salade niçoise. Previous MasterChef winners Jane Devonshire, Dr Saliha Mahmood Ahmed and Thomas Frake assess their efforts

programme.title: LS Lowry: The Unheard Tapes
programme.desc: An insight into the secretive 'matchstick men' painter as he guides viewers through his revealing life story in unheard interviews from the end of his life. Recorded by a young fan, Angela Barratt, in 1972, the tapes uncover much about Lowry's life story and the changing north he captured in his iconic paintings. Lip-synced by actors Ian McKellen and Annabel Smith.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The quiz heads to Cardiff as Vernon Kay, with Paul Gorton in tow, tests TV icon Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and Radio 1Xtra DJ Remi Burgz on their UK knowledge

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps mother and daughter Diana and Millie seek out space to bloom in the Somerset countryside and also meets a couple who have planted their own vineyard

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure in Uzbekistan continues in Samarkand, one of the oldest cities in Central Asia. He discovers the national passion for bread, leading him to a family bakery where he learns how to bake special, bagel-like loaves. The great conqueror, Tamerlane, made Samarkand his capital in the 14th-century, and at his tomb Michael learns of the brutality and military prowess of the man who is today Uzbekistan's national hero

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The team visits the RHS Malvern Spring Festival in Worcestershire, where Rachel de Thame and Joe Swift share everything the show has to offer. Gardens, plants and expert advice, as well as tips and tricks to try at home. There is a visit to the Devon-Somerset border to meet a grower who, along with the help of a couple of feathered friends, has transformed a woodland landscape into a stunning nursery. And one designer explains why she is creating her first show to demonstrate the art of pressing flowers

programme.title: Impossible

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the fourth heat of the classic quiz in which contenders take on the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to become the next Mastermind Champion. Tonight's specialist subjects are Detectorists, the Alice books of Lewis Carroll, British politics in the 1960s, and Elton John

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It

programme.title: Super Smart Animals
programme.desc: Part one of two. Liz Bonnin sets off on a quest to find the world's most intelligent animals. She has a creative experience with dolphins, meets an octopus escapologist and challenges a chimpanzee to a maths and memory test. Her journey takes her to Los Angeles where she meets Tillman, a skateboarding dog, and John Humphrys joins in by finding out how a goldfish would cope on Mastermind

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team seek out more items of rarity, heading for the gardens of Knightshayes Court in Devon, where they find a copy of a blue-blooded play with royalty on the cast list, a collection of jewellery in a gold-tooled leather box, and a huge pair of rare Doulton vases decorated with kangaroos

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour through Uzbekistan continues in the lush oasis surrounding the historic city of Samarkand. Here, he uncovers the Central Asian origins of the apple, a fruit that travelled to Europe along the Silk Road. Boarding a high-speed train along the old Trans-Caspian Railway, Michael also hears how the Russians toiled to build this remarkable line through the shifting sands of Uzbekistan's steppe

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Emma suspects the pond is being fished by more than their local fishermen and trail cameras capture the visit of a family of hungry otters. The Williamsons take their ram lambs to a prestigious sale on the mainland hoping to make big money and secure their reputation as top tup breeders, while Callum and Zara's children Lucy and Lottie enter the young handlers competition at the Arran agricultural show

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last heat of this year's competition has arrived, and the final group of six amateurs have been waiting for their shot at culinary stardom. Anna Haugh and Grace Dent will be putting them through their paces across two tasks. The hopefuls include two teachers, a pub manager and a civil servant. The signature dish round kicks off proceedings, with Anna and Grace hoping to sample a creation that represents the amateurs down to a tee

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Porthcawl's crew receive a call about a swimmer who's been swept out to sea in a rip current on the south-west coast of Wales. On the Cornish coast, the Sennen Cove and Penlee crews respond to reports of a 31,000 ton cargo ship that's about to run aground on the Wolf Rock lighthouse. The Chiswick crew race to rescue of a party boat that has lost engine power on the Thames

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Officers are called to a house where a woman has been punched in the face and find themselves confronted by a man with a large knife. There is a tense stand-off before they are able to cuff and arrest him. Dave and Owen come across a group of drug users openly injecting cocaine in broad daylight, while other officers are called to reports of a street fight between two women

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O'Connell

Also, surgeon Malcolm Will has taken on a rare case involving a tumour that has invaded his patient's chest wall

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London, tackling an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charity's ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers, before Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty flails as Ning continues to push him. Meanwhile, Leanne and Gary bond, until he pressures her to betray Matty to safeguard her family

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps Carol, who wants a home near the sea in south-west Wales for her and her husband. Accompanied by her son Greg, Carol dreams of a picture-perfect double-fronted house with roses around the door. Can Nicki deliver the property she's after?

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour through Uzbekistan continues in the lush oasis surrounding the historic city of Samarkand. Here, he uncovers the Central Asian origins of the apple, a fruit that travelled to Europe along the Silk Road. Boarding a high-speed train along the old Trans-Caspian Railway, Michael also hears how the Russians toiled to build this remarkable line through the shifting sands of Uzbekistan's steppe

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie heads to Kent, where he meets soon-to-be-grandmother Tracey and suggests to her that borrowing a pet might lower her blood pressure. He explains how spending time with animals has been proven to boost happiness, improve wellbeing and lower the risk of dying from cardiovascular disease

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan completes this leg of his journey along Ireland's Wild Atlantic in County Derry/Londonderry. He walks the 400-year-old walls of the city of Derry/Londonderry with comedian Diona Doherty. He also hits the farm to find out what it takes to be a competitive sheep herder, before heading for the windswept coastline of Castlerock to meet a skilled craft worker who builds traditional coracle boats by hand

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: Cameras capture the world from fresh and exhilarating perspectives. This episode features an underwater find from a violent past, a mysterious concrete 'ghost island' and never-before-seen tracks along the seabed

programme.title: Close Encounters Down Under
programme.desc: A visit to the land beyond the coastal fringe, to the vast expanses of Australia where cattle outnumbers people, but those who live here have extraordinary UFO stories to tell

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Investigating whether spiritual leaders known as shamans, who have healed and protected people throughout history really have contact with extraterrestrial realms

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: American couple and civil rights activists Betty and Barney Hill, who claimed they were abducted by extraterrestrials in a rural area of New Hampshire in September 1961. What really happened to the hills that night? Was this a real case of aliens among us or a couple just looking for attention?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Broadhaven is a sleepy community in south-west Wales, but since the late 1970s it has made headlines for being the UFO capital of Europe. In 1977, schoolchildren spotted a cigar-shaped aircraft. The sighting transforms the quiet seaside town into a UFO hot spot

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The Golds have a decision to make when a man wants to buy a pair of very expensive earrings on the condition that the store also does his piercing

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Former employee Tressa returns to sell the Rolex that got her sacked and Les struggles to control the situation when insults start to fly between her and Ashley

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A stressed Seth blames his father for chaos in the shop, but amid the angry clients a couple bring in one of the strangest objects the family has ever seen

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team travel to Ludlow, Shropshire, to value antiques. David Hakeney, Helen Gardiner, Corrie Jeffery and David Ford examine goods including a watch that has a connection to Margaret Thatcher and a Scottish vase

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team value antiques in Stamford, Lincolnshire, where two sisters give dealer David Tupman a run for his money. Meanwhile, expert Henry Nicholls tries to purchase a diamond ring, but the owner considers taking it to auction

programme.title: Pawn Stars Do America
programme.desc: Rick Harrison, Corey Harrison and Austin 'Chumlee' Russell are ready to mess with Texas, as they take on Austin. With the help of a few trusted companions, they're hoping to round up some gems.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces appraise The Greatest Auction, Our Planet, Countdown, M3GAN, Siren: Survive the Island, Gino's Italian masterclass on This Morning, and Trooping the Colour on the News. Among those offering their thoughts are Ricky Tomlinson, Sue Johnston, Adam Lambert, Beverley Knight, Denise van Outen, Melanie C and Shaun Ryder. From June 2023

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Penny is in anaphylactic shock after a wasp sting and Warwickshire, Northamptonshire, Derbyshire, Leicestershire and Rutland air ambulance crews are on their way. The Essex and Herts air ambulance look after teenager Connor, who has broken his leg while doing tricks on his bike. Plus, motocross rider Carl needs Thames Valley air ambulance's help after an accident leaves him with a dislocated hip

programme.title: Wonders of the World I Can't See
programme.desc: Chris McCausland visits Jordan in the Middle East with actor, writer and comedian Guz Khan, to visit the magnificent ancient city of Petra. But before they head to the wonder, Chris floats for the first time in his life in the Dead Sea, and the pair almost melt during an intense facial spa treatment. In Petra itself, they haggle with shopkeepers, ride camels and listen to tales told by candlelight, before ending their trip with traditional music and a Bedouin feast

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: Sandi is joined by comedian Judi Love for a tour of the Lake District as they stay in the best staycation hideaways they can find. Starting at the Gaia Farmhouse by Ullswater, Judi enjoys the sights and sounds of the season and Sandi feels the heat when Judi cooks an exceedingly spicy take on cottage pie. The pair then head to the Lingholm Estate to stay in a unique boathouse that floats over a wild and stormy Derwentwater and go for a very choppy boat ride. The adventure concludes on the banks of Windermere in Grey Gables where Sandi and Judi unwind with some wine-time and homemade watercolour postcards

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his two-part challenge at Mangia Mangia in Woodland Park, Colorado. In order to save the struggling Italian restaurant from disaster, he must persuade harassed owner Julie to co-operate with the head chef, and work his magic on a menu that relies heavily on a microwave

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners from Derby take part in the dinner competition, hoping to secure the £1,000 prize. Then, at the third party, Dave and Gemma are convinced their organisation in the kitchen will bag them the jackpot - but a guest's belly dancing threatens to overshadow their efforts

programme.desc: A caller to his radio show gives Frasier the impression Niles's wife Maris might be having an affair with her fencing instructor. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.desc: Bebe advises Frasier to feign illness to secure a pay rise and prove he is indispensable, but the station responds by cleaning out his cubicle and auditioning for a replacement. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Harriet Sansom Harris

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Crane brothers buy into a restaurant, but their conflicting views on everything leave them without a head chef on opening night - and most of the waiters also walk out. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin prepares to celebrate his birthday, but an argument between Niles and Frasier over a patient, as well as a city-wide power cut, ensures none of the household is in the mood for a party. Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce star

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to New Orleans to visit Zeke's, a much-loved restaurant that has seen standards slip since the original owner died. The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Gemma Longworth's restorations include a 1947 Harley-Davidson and a redundant aeroplane seat

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: An unexpected delivery causes chaos at a medieval village in France. Meanwhile, Francesca and Carl, the owners of a hilltop hamlet in Italy, get started on a brand-new project

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Rory Bremner makes an impression in Dictionary Corner as he joins Colin Murray and Rachel Riley for the words-and-numbers game

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Keen cyclists Debbie and Graham from Portsmouth first got together nearly 30 years ago and now want to find their very own holiday home in Spain. With family and friends lining up for weekend visits, they've chosen the town of Oliva, in the south of Spain's Valencia region. Sara Damergi conducts a rigorous search to come up with five options within their £80,000 budget

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Sidhbh and Neasa prepare their new plastic surgery clinic in Miami for its first clients. Laura and Lindsay steel themselves for the eyewatering cost of childbirth in Florida. David and Gina finally open their refurbished holiday rentals for business

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul plan to install a rear balcony at their chateau in Brittany, but it's a demanding job that requires Paul to handle heavy steel girders and cut through decades-old reinforced concrete. Renovations are also under way at Châteaux de la Vergne and de la Grillère, while a wedding party are trapped by dangerous trees near Cognac

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sharon and Shaun Garner help Clayton, Abigail and Harry search for a holiday home in Almería that can become a permanent home when 14-year-old Harry flies the nest and in Costa Blanca, Jo and Andrew Alderton help a couple who are looking to escape the weather of West Yorkshire

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples find homes in Leeds. Emily and Lucy, a paramedic and a nurse, are spoilt for choice when Kirstie gives them multiple attractive options that are convenient for the hospital where they both work. Sarah and Tom, meanwhile, are currently living apart and challenge Phil to find the perfect balance of indoor and outdoor space so they can move on with their lives together

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The documentary picks up four months after the attempted assassination, when there was still no sign of the source of the Novichok. Salisbury re-opened after months under lockdown - only for civilians Dawn Sturgess and Charlie Rowley to fall ill in nearby Amesbury. As the death of the former turned it into a murder investigation, questions began to be asked about how well the UK reacted to such unprecedented Russian aggression and the stark security lessons that were learnt

programme.title: Do You Know This Man?
programme.desc: The story of two women who, as teenagers in the 1990s, were groomed and sexually abused by a friend's father. Years later, as adults, the women recognised what had been done to them and reported it to police. But the case collapsed in court due to a procedural error and their abuser walked free. So Laura posted a public appeal on social media for other victims to come forward. In 2018, a third woman responded. This documentary traces three survivors' fight for justice, revealing the reality of grooming and the trauma of being disbelieved

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Jamie's Ultimate BBQ, Britain's Got Talent, Widow's Bay, Married at First Sight Australia, Great Korean Railway Journeys, Apex and the BBC News report on the flatpack championships

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: It's a family affair at the restaurant as Fred's parents come for lunch. Twenty-six-year-old Jordan arrives for a double date with his mum Kelly. Jordan hits it off with Aimee, but will mum watching from afar throw them off? Kelly's date is with carpenter and triathlete John, while Ukrainian single mum Kateryna meets warm-hearted Peter, and sparks fly over shared values, laughter and a few heartfelt toasts in Ukrainian

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end

programme.title: Plan B (2021)
programme.desc: After a regrettable first sexual encounter, a straight-laced high school student and her slacker best friend have 24 hours to hunt down a morning after pill in America's heartland. Comedy, starring Kuhoo Verma and Victoria Moroles

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woody turns to Sam's little black book for help in his search for a girlfriend, so the would-be Lothario tries to come up trumps. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Frasier tries to boost Diane's confidence by praising her terrible dancing, unwittingly prompting her to audition for the Boston ballet. Meanwhile, Cliff buys a new Italian sports car and sells his clapped-out station wagon to Woody. Comedy, starring Shelley Long and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam finally asks Diane to marry him, but she rejects his proposal - making him so angry that he chases her out of the bar and ends up in court charged with assault and battery. Ted Danson and Shelley Long star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is heartbroken to discover her parents have decided to split up and go their separate ways. Comedy, starring Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles suggests he and Frasier set up practice together - but after a series of disasters, they end up in need of therapy themselves. Comedy, guest starring Milo O'Shea, with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The family prepares to mourn a relative's death, and while Frasier and Niles organise the funeral in their usual hopeless style, Martin tries to finish a song he began writing for Frank Sinatra. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Kate encourages Bulldog to continue his audience-grabbing practical jokes against Frasier, who decides to exact a suitable revenge - blissfully unaware that such pranks have a nasty habit of backfiring. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Dan Butler

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole explore some old chicken sheds piled high with stuff in Lincolnshire, finding a toilet cistern and a dressing room mirror to fix up

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: Francesca and Carl race against time to reopen their Italian hamlet for business. Meanwhile, a British-run restored medieval village suffers some damage after a big party, and the owners of a derelict French settlement come to terms with the need to demolish some of its buildings

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Rory Bremner joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner on the words-and-numbers game, chaired by Colin Murray

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Cumbria couple Samantha and Sam want to find their dream holiday home in Spain that works for them and their teenage sons. With a budget of £85,000, the pair hop Lee can deliver their Spanish dream

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Attendance at Pastor Simon's church declined during Covid. He's hoping an Easter Sunday sunrise service on his local Atlantic beach will boost his congregation, but it looks like it'll take a miracle to get everything organised in time. Adam and Wayne attempt to put on a live show of their successful Fish'n'Brits podcast to celebrate its first year on air. Laura and Lindsay celebrate the arrival of their son Elliott with a family outing to the Universal theme park where they were married 10 years ago

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Jane and Rory's handmade kitchen island won't fit through the gîte door at Château de Pican. Bart and Sarah rush to prepare their Cognac barn for their best friend's wedding, and at Château de la Vergne, Lionel and Claire work on the gilded ceiling for their dining room and bespoke fromage cupboard

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, Rodriguez dream team Charlotte and brother-in-law Damon search for a property within walking distance of a beach for Northamptonshire couple Steve and Amanda. In Murcia, Natalie Brown and sales manager Charlie are on the hunt for a family friendly holiday home for property developer Steve and his partner Hannah, fun-loving workaholics from Yorkshire

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: The funnyman rides the rails of eastern Germany, where he finds himself in the cab of an unusual electric coal-transporting train and marvels at the ingenuity of the prisoners of Colditz. From there he takes a 'car on rails' made from a Trabant, dives underground into a silver mine by electric train, drives a huge 19th-century locomotive - his biggest steam train yet - and drives a modern tram in Dresden while taking in the rebuilt city's architecture

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Amy Gledhill gets creative with a series of spoons. Kumail Nanjiani impresses everyone with his extraordinarily strong jaw and Alex Horne causes Joanna Page to scream in such terror that it sparks alarm in the local area. Armando Iannucci douses a coconut in vinegar, while Joel Dommett slathers another in glue. Greg Davies hands out the points

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, as Roisin Conaty and Phil Wang compete against Joe Wilkinson and Lou Sanders. Regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley and lexicographer Susie Dent are on hand to adjudicate, while David O'Doherty joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: TFI Friday Unplugged
programme.desc: Chris Evans presents more chat and music, featuring Bob Odenkirk, Danny Dyer, Emilia Clarke, Nigel Ng, Tom Ogden and Joe Donovan from Blossoms, and Gary Lightbody

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The participants are encouraged to explore their sexual fantasies, from submission and dominance to bondage and impact play. The group begin experimenting with each other and there is also some erotic story writing. Millie, Ed and Will begin to discover new desires and confidence, while others, like Bertie, question whether this part of the programme is suited to them

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The group explore how basic skills can transform intimacy. In the workshop, they practise kissing, which is exciting for some but daunting for others. Ellen gets naked with surrogate partner Kat, but struggles with intimate touch. Alex and Joy aim to finally conquer their individual issues as their relationship grows closer, while Shelby unlocks Millie's buried emotions and desires

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his visit to Zayna Flaming Grill in Redondo Beach, California, and finds more questions than answers as he tries to save the crisis-ridden restaurant from disaster. programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
