The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Gemma Longworth's restorations include a 1947 Harley-Davidson and a redundant aeroplane seat

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: An unexpected delivery causes chaos at a medieval village in France. Meanwhile, Francesca and Carl, the owners of a hilltop hamlet in Italy, get started on a brand-new project

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Rory Bremner makes an impression in Dictionary Corner as he joins Colin Murray and Rachel Riley for the words-and-numbers game

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Keen cyclists Debbie and Graham from Portsmouth first got together nearly 30 years ago and now want to find their very own holiday home in Spain. With family and friends lining up for weekend visits, they've chosen the town of Oliva, in the south of Spain's Valencia region. Sara Damergi conducts a rigorous search to come up with five options within their £80,000 budget

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Sidhbh and Neasa prepare their new plastic surgery clinic in Miami for its first clients. Laura and Lindsay steel themselves for the eyewatering cost of childbirth in Florida. David and Gina finally open their refurbished holiday rentals for business

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul plan to install a rear balcony at their chateau in Brittany, but it's a demanding job that requires Paul to handle heavy steel girders and cut through decades-old reinforced concrete. Renovations are also under way at Châteaux de la Vergne and de la Grillère, while a wedding party are trapped by dangerous trees near Cognac

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sharon and Shaun Garner help Clayton, Abigail and Harry search for a holiday home in Almería that can become a permanent home when 14-year-old Harry flies the nest and in Costa Blanca, Jo and Andrew Alderton help a couple who are looking to escape the weather of West Yorkshire

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples find homes in Leeds. Emily and Lucy, a paramedic and a nurse, are spoilt for choice when Kirstie gives them multiple attractive options that are convenient for the hospital where they both work. Sarah and Tom, meanwhile, are currently living apart and challenge Phil to find the perfect balance of indoor and outdoor space so they can move on with their lives together

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The documentary picks up four months after the attempted assassination, when there was still no sign of the source of the Novichok. Salisbury re-opened after months under lockdown - only for civilians Dawn Sturgess and Charlie Rowley to fall ill in nearby Amesbury. As the death of the former turned it into a murder investigation, questions began to be asked about how well the UK reacted to such unprecedented Russian aggression and the stark security lessons that were learnt

programme.title: Do You Know This Man?
programme.desc: The story of two women who, as teenagers in the 1990s, were groomed and sexually abused by a friend's father. Years later, as adults, the women recognised what had been done to them and reported it to police. But the case collapsed in court due to a procedural error and their abuser walked free. So Laura posted a public appeal on social media for other victims to come forward. In 2018, a third woman responded. This documentary traces three survivors' fight for justice, revealing the reality of grooming and the trauma of being disbelieved

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces appraise The Greatest Auction, Our Planet, Countdown, M3GAN, Siren: Survive the Island, Gino's Italian masterclass on This Morning, and Trooping the Colour on the News. Among those offering their thoughts are Ricky Tomlinson, Sue Johnston, Adam Lambert, Beverley Knight, Denise van Outen, Melanie C and Shaun Ryder. From June 2023

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The participants are encouraged to explore their sexual fantasies, from submission and dominance to bondage and impact play. The group begin experimenting with each other and there is also some erotic story writing. Millie, Ed and Will begin to discover new desires and confidence, while others, like Bertie, question whether this part of the programme is suited to them

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end

programme.desc: The funnyman rides the rails of eastern Germany, where he finds himself in the cab of an unusual electric coal-transporting train and marvels at the ingenuity of the prisoners of Colditz. From there he takes a 'car on rails' made from a Trabant, dives underground into a silver mine by electric train, drives a huge 19th-century locomotive - his biggest steam train yet - and drives a modern tram in Dresden while taking in the rebuilt city's architecture

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Home Study

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Late Thanksgiving

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Birth Mother
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler travel to Ohio to meet the mother of the baby they plan to adopt. The pregnant mother, Erica, likes them but only because their files got mixed up with a doctor and a female minister. Meanwhile, Joey is set up on a date with one of Phoebe's friends, but soon regrets it when she tries to take some food off his plate, and Ross has a fashion faux pas when he accidently wears women's clothing on a date

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Gets Caught
programme.desc: Rachel and Phoebe see Chandler getting into a car with a mystery woman and follow them to a large house in the suburbs. They tell Monica of their fears and are shocked to hear the truth. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston, Lisa Kudrow and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where the Stripper Cries
programme.desc: Joey appears on quiz show The $100,000 Pyramid, but his disastrous efforts to answer the questions end up costing his partner the prize money. Meanwhile, the revellers at Phoebe's hen party get a surprise once the stripper shows up. Guest starring Danny DeVito and Donny Osmond

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's big day arrives, but wedding planner Monica and stand-in father of the bride Joey take their roles a little too seriously. To make matters worse, a blizzard hits New York and one of Mike's ushers is indisposed, forcing Ross and Chandler to step into the breach

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Speaks French
programme.desc: Joey persuades Phoebe to teach him French for an important audition, while Monica and Chandler show a pregnant Erica the sights of New York and learn more about the baby's possible father. Guest starring Anna Faris and Ron Leibman

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel heads to a restaurant for a job interview, only to find herself sitting at a table within earshot of her current boss. Meanwhile, Monica and Chandler go house-hunting and Phoebe decides to change her name. Guest starring Paul Rudd and Dakota Fanning

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe learns Joey's agent has died, but he is so upset about his friends moving away that she decides not to tell him. programme.title: Friends

Meanwhile, Monica supervises the packing for a new life in the suburbs with Chandler, while also keeping a close eye on the mother of their future adopted child

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The Last One (Parts One and Two)

Jennifer Aniston, Courteney Cox, Lisa Kudrow, Matt LeBlanc, Matthew Perry and David Schwimmer star

programme.title: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery (1997)

Spoof spy adventure, with Mike Myers, Elizabeth Hurley, Mimi Rogers, Michael York and Robert Wagner

Things liven up when the two families split into teams of Native Americans and Pilgrims to mark the national holiday. Comedy, starring Brad Garrett and Monica Horan

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The kids damage Ray's dictionary, prompting Frank to tell everyone about the time Ray ruined his prized jazz collection. However, he discovers that he may not have been the culprit all along

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra helps to plan a recruitment party at Frank's lodge, leading Ray to grumble about how much time she is spending away from home. Comedy, starring Patricia Hearten and Ray Romano

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Memorial Service
programme.desc: A joke entry on Ross's college website backfires on Chandler. Joey refuses to give Emma his favourite stuffed toy, and weak-willed Phoebe begs Monica to help her avoid contacting Mike. Comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone except Ross pools their money to buy lottery tickets, but a disagreement about how to spend any winnings prompts a disdainful Phoebe to make a rash gesture. Chandler frets as he waits for a phone call that could mean a new job, and Rachel thinks Emma is close to saying her first word

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel joins Joey on the set of his soap opera, where a steamy love scene inspires unexpected feelings - and a disturbing dream. Phoebe takes an opportunity to serenade a captive audience, while Chandler plans to whisk Monica away - but ends up inviting Ross. Jennifer Aniston and Matt LeBlanc star

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Soap Opera Party
programme.desc: Joey throws a party for the cast of Days of Our Lives, but refuses to invite his pals in case their behaviour embarrasses him. However, the answering machine gives the game away and everyone attends - which is good news for Ross

programme.title: Jimmy Carr's Am I the A**hole?
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, GK Barry and Jamali Maddix pass judgement on a man who faked having a baby, an unrepentant snorer and a brutal date dodger

programme.title: Bradley & Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his 20-year-old son Barney hit the road together, travelling more than 2,000 miles from Los Angeles to New Orleans. The pair begin their journey in Los Angeles, and head down to Muscle Beach to lift weights, before taking part in a game of volleyball. The following day, Bradley is terrified to hear that the agenda involves a 14,000ft skydive

programme.title: Bradley & Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Actor Bradley Walsh and his 20-year-old son Barney hit the road together, travelling more than 2,000 miles from Los Angeles to New Orleans. In the second leg of their journey, the pair leave the city of Los Angeles behind and make their way down the famous Route 66 to the southwestern state of Arizona. Here, they ride through a deserted ghost town, before the father and son reach Lake Havasu City, where Barney has arranged for them to try jet-skiing. Finally, they attend a Cowboy College, where they learn to become real-life horsemen

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The comedian welcomes guests Kylie Minogue and Bradley Walsh for an evening of entertainment at London's Theatre Royal on Drury Lane. Kylie performs a ballad with Jack Savoretti, and is joined onstage by a group of audience members for a special karaoke challenge, while Bradley plays Celebrity Send to All, in which Michael hijacks his mobile phone to send an embarrassing message to all his recorded contacts. Plus, an airline pilot is the Unexpected Star as his West End dreams come true

programme.title: Utopia
programme.desc: Jim and Rhonda force Tony to step in to save a flawed government scheme, while Nat is driven mad by a worried local council and the entire office prepares for the staff talent show. Return of the Australian comedy, starring Rob Sitch

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler conduct an embarrassing interview with a potential sperm donor after learning they cannot conceive a baby naturally. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Double bill. The gang visits Barbados to hear Ross address a convention, but Chandler manages to destroy the only copy of his keynote speech. Joey and Rachel impersonate pharmacists to infiltrate a medical conference offering free drinks, while Mike arrives unexpectedly and proposes to Phoebe

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Joey and Rachel Kiss

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Is Fine
programme.desc: Ross tries to put on a brave face and invites Joey and Rachel to dinner with him and Charlie, but gets drunk and makes an embarrassing scene. Frank Jr tries to talk Phoebe into taking care of one of the triplets, while Chandler and Monica learn more about adoption

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross goes to a spray-tanning salon, but his failure to follow the instructions leaves him with a complexion he was not looking for. Joey and Rachel prepare to sleep together for the first time, but the night proves more awkward than romantic. Guest starring Jennifer Coolidge

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler decide to delay their trip to Vermont to celebrate Emma's first birthday, but the party takes an unexpected turn with the delivery of a risque birthday cake. Comedy, guest starring Elliott Gould

programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel's Sister Babysits

programme.sub-title: The Last One (Parts One and Two)

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Part one. The experts seek clarity on the burning issues one final time, with some heartbreaking truths laid bare

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: On Valentine's night, 50-year-old Dave, a singer and wedding celebrant who unites couples in matrimonial bliss, hopes to find a piece of that bliss for himself. A 66-year-old poet from West Yorkshire has memory problems after a serious motorbike accident. His date is a grandmother of six, who inspires him to write a poem on the spot. A 29-year-old artist and a mother of one enjoy one of the flirtiest encounters the restaurant has ever seen

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Apu's mother arrives in Springfield to organise his arranged marriage to the daughter of a family friend, but he is unwilling to have his future decided for him and pretends he already has a wife - Marge

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa goes on an archaeological dig with the school, and uncovers an unusual fossil, which the townsfolk are convinced is the skeleton of an angel. Thinking he can make a profit out of the find, Homer steals it, puts it in his garage, and charges people to see it. With the guest voice of scientist Stephen Jay Gould

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Nerves jangle as Luke and Manny face their first day at high school. Cam takes on a substitute gig, leaving Mitch to look after Lily. American comedy, starring Eric Stonestreet and Jesse Tyler Ferguson

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Gloria panics when Joe is deemed too aggressive for gym class, leading her to worry that he has inherited a family curse, and Cam spins Lily a little white lie about her missing cat, only for it to spiral out of control. Phil taps into a rich new vein of clients - recently divorced women - but are they more trouble than they're worth? American comedy, starring Sofia Vergara, Eric Stonestreet and Ty Burrell

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer wins $1million on the lottery, but keeps it a secret from his family, fearing Marge's reaction if she finds out about it. He and Bart set about spending the money, and buy front-row tickets to a Coldplay concert. With the guest voice of Chris Martin

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is horrified at Marge's attempts to make him befriend Ralph Wiggum - until he learns his police chief dad has a master key to every shop in Springfield. In a bid to impress Nelson and his bully buddies, Bart and Ralph break into an abandoned prison, where they find an electric chair

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Guilt-stricken at having spent all the family's Christmas money on himself, Homer resolves to turn over a new leaf and become a do-gooder - but his kindness soon starts to rival that of Ned Flanders, and the neighbours embark on a battle for supremacy

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer enters Lisa in a beauty pageant to boost her self-esteem, and has the satisfaction of seeing her crowned runner-up - but she finds her principles heavily compromised as a result. Featuring the voice of Bob Hope

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Part two. The experts seek clarity on the burning issues one final time, with some heartbreaking truths laid bare

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Content creator and footballer PK Humble heads into the agency to provide Anna and Dr Tara with a challenge. Stephen is set up with a funeral arranger

programme.title: MAFS Australia: After the Dinner Party
programme.desc: All of the gossip following Final Vows and the reunion. Bec is feeling blindsided, behind-the-scenes footage exposes a new side to Danny, and there is yet another texting scandal on the horizon

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dairy farmer Ben hopes to find romance with marketing assistant Alice, while classical music singer Zoe dines with amateur dramatics enthusiast Tim. The restaurant also welcomes Sara, who has been single for eight years. Could straight-talking animal lover and dog trainer Nicky be the right woman for her?

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Part two. The experts seek clarity on the burning issues one final time, with some heartbreaking truths laid bare

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Content creator and footballer PK Humble heads into the agency to provide Anna and Dr Tara with a challenge. Stephen is set up with a funeral arranger

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer wins $1million on the lottery, but keeps it a secret from his family, fearing Marge's reaction if she finds out about it. He and Bart set about spending the money, and buy front-row tickets to a Coldplay concert. With the guest voice of Chris Martin

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A romantic evening at the ice-rink leads to Homer and Marge heading for the Winter Olympics to play for a curling team, while Lisa becomes hopelessly addicted to collecting sporting memorabilia and Bart befriends Canada's equivalent of Milhouse

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly is pursued for a first date, while Adam questions his friendship with Dave Kim

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly's child-rearing tactics appear unorthodox, while Barry and Adam concoct a plan to become more independent from their mother

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Hapless Liam wants his greatest passion to take flight with a darts-inspired wedding, but controlling bride-to-be Chelsey dreams of a much more traditional affair

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil finds himself in an awkward situation when he meets his former high-school girlfriend, who confesses she still has feelings for him, while Mitchell stands up to his boss and Jay summons up the courage to confess he killed Manny's turtle

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Claire help out with their children's school projects, while Gloria and Cameron spend quality time together. Elsewhere, Mitchell and Manny learn they have more in common than they thought during a trip to watch a meteor shower with Jay. Comedy, starring Ty Burrell and Julie Bowen

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Comedy clips showcasing amusing internet videos on the themes of food and animals

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Love and loyalty win when Lainey comes home, and Adam and Carmen come to a crossroads in their relationship. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly struggles with her feelings for a new crush while Barry leases his own apartment without considering Joanne. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire flies solo to her college reunion and runs into a professor she once dated, but when Phil shows up things get really awkward. Back at home, Jay is obsessed with winning his big bowling tournament

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire and Cam fall out while trying to sell the house they bought together as an investment, so the latter brings in his pal Pam (Wendi McLendon-Covey, Bridesmaids) to help out. Phil asks his dad for advice after realising how dependent his kids have become, while Jay thinks he's made a lucky escape when he trades in a book-reading with Manny for a day out with Joe - only to discover a trip to the cinema isn't such a great idea with a crying baby - and Mitch tries to settle an argument between Lily and classmate Milo

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Part two. The experts seek clarity on the burning issues one final time, with some heartbreaking truths laid bare

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: TikTok content creator Holly enters the restaurant looking for love and ends up paired with landscaper Sam, who shares her love of tequila. Journalist and broadcaster India Willoughby - a self-proclaimed Bridget Jones - goes on her first date since she transitioned, with driving instructor Gareth

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes Ellie Simmonds into her kitchen, with the five-time Paralympic swimming gold medallist making banana bread with chocolate chips, before the Bake Off judge takes a trip down memory lane with bobotie, an old favourite from her South African childhood. Prue's husband John heads to a local cheesemaker to sample some of their produce before joining his wife back in the kitchen, where she makes brie and blackberries with chilli jelly on soda bread

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates how sometimes simple cookery ideas are the best with clever recipes that make the most of his weekly shop but use just one pan. Dishes include herby artichoke stew, chicken in cider and compost crumble

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer turns the contents of his weekly shop into seven more meals, showing how to make ingredients go further - from getting the most out of a cut of ribs to using every last vegetable peeling. Dishes include butternut squash soup with crisps, rib ragout and cherry chocolate iced rice

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond prepares refreshing strawberry cherry lemon limeade and orange bars for her family hauling in hay in high summer

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond prepares a stuffed pizza crust with arrabiata sauce, and orange blueberry muffin tops

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver goes on a mission to change the way people think about vegetables, demonstrating a collection of vibrant, hearty and healthy vegetarian dishes that everyone can add to their kitchen. In the first edition, he kicks things off with a cottage pie with a rich gravy and super-fluffy mash, is inspired by Indian street food, then makes a chip butty packed full of flavour and crunch. He goes back to school to meet children who are celebrating vegetables, and makes macaroni and cheese full of greens

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates an easy scruffy aubergine lasagna, topped with a crispy sage and almond crunch, and also explores the produce in Asia's biggest fruit and vegetable market - inspiring him to make a bean salad with lots of punch

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina offers tips and creative ideas for cooking with sweet and savoury pastry. She makes fig tart, spinach in puff pastry and warm goat's cheese in filo

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.sub-title: Cook Like A Pro: Portable Food
programme.desc: Ina shares her useful and easy recipes for portable food, and she prepares some of her favourite like lemon poppy seed cake and a tasty provencal sandwich

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig present the semi-final of the baking contest, which tests the four remaining bakers' skills with patisserie. There are only three places in next week's final, and this round of challenges will determine who does not make the cut. The signature dish requires a delicate dome and a perfect finish, while for the technical, the bakers must take croquembouche to the next level. Finally, for the showstopper, they must use all their skills to construct an edible display case

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: The food writer and Great British Bake Off judge gives step-by-step instructions for the perfect Sunday lunch as she cooks for three generations of her family. For the main course she prepares slow-roasted shoulder of lamb with rosemary and paprika rub, served with potato and fennel gratin, and dishes up plum tarte Tatin with fruit salad for dessert

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by fellow chef Tony Singh for a tour of the Isle of Skye. Tony begins by visiting a local bakery that doubles as an art gallery. He also learns how to make the local oatcakes. James, meanwhile, is inspired by the seafood to cook a dish using plaice and lovage. The duo then take a boat trip, where they go dolphin spotting, and James cooks up a fresh lobster and langoustine dish with an added Scottish twist

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James visits the Shetland Islands, an area of natural beauty that he's always wanted to see. He gets to hire a vintage car to explore the area, where he learns about traditional fish salting and cooks a tasty vegetable curry at a local social enterprise project. Later on, James learns all about Shetland community spirit and cake at one of their famous Sunday teas, and finally cooks up a Shetland-inspired paella on a beautiful local beach

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates more vibrant, hearty and healthy vegetarian dishes that everyone can add to their menus, finding inspiration from across the country and around the globe. In this edition he puts vegetables centre stage with two recipes for the price of one as he makes a flavour-packed asparagus quiche accompanied by a silky soup, then uses leeks to give his carbonara a luxurious veggie twist . In the spice shops of Jerusalem, he seeks meat-free inspiration and cooks up a show-stopping cauliflower roast

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas takes over from Sandi Toksvig as host for the 2020 series, alongside returning presenter Noel Fielding and regular judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith. The 12 contestants for this series have been required to form a social bubble due to Covid-19 lockdown restrictions. The contest begins with cake week, beginning with a signature challenge to produce a classic Battenberg. For the technical round, Paul sets the contestants the task of making a fruit sponge, and the final showstopper challenge gives the bakers a chance to express their creativity

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Part two of two. Klinger is accused of stealing a camera, and decides to have Major Winchester represent him in court. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda, David Ogden Stiers and Jamie Farr

programme.title: M*A*S*H

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: Rumpole of the Bailey
programme.sub-title: Rumpole and the Miscarriage of Justice
programme.desc: A mentally challenged man confesses to the murder of a respected policeman in a street riot. However, evidence surfaces that part of the confession has been altered. Judge Guthrie Featherstone presides at the subsequent trial of a detective superintendent and has to contend with Rumpole for the defence, but during this stressful time makes the unwise decision to escort legal clerk Dot Clapton to a club. Legal drama, starring Leo McKern and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Ballykissangel
programme.desc: The residents are thrown into panic by the arrival of two snooping strangers who everyone assumes are from the Revenue Commission - a suspicion seemingly confirmed by the theft of Quigley's dodgy accounts. Stephen Tompkinson, Dervla Kirwan and Tony Doyle star

programme.title: Down to Earth
programme.desc: The rural idyll is somewhat disturbed by Ricky Tomlinson as new arrival Tony Murphy, the wheeler-dealing pub landlord who is in for the shock of his life. Back at Silverdale, the Brewer family struggle with the day-to-day business of the farm and the news of Frankie's pregnancy, but more disruption comes in the form of glamorous stranger Paula who, while spending the night at the B&B, reveals she is on the run from her violent ex-boyfriend. Claire King guest stars, with Angela Griffin, Ian Kelsey and Denise Welch

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Robert tries to arrange a love-match between Eleanor and one of his old comrades-in-arms, but meets with a conspicuous lack of success. Then a belligerent boxer with head injuries gives him an unexpected helping hand, and medicine starts taking a back seat to romance at the Thrift. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave, David Calder and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Rumpole of the Bailey
programme.desc: The barrister attends a concert performed by the Casterini Trio. programme.desc: A teenager at a new school befriends a pretty girl, but her ex-boyfriend is jealous and attacks him. programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: In October 1989 nine-year-old Jessi Toronjo went to a sleepover in 1989 thinking that her stepbrother, Jeffrey Pelley, was on his way to his senior prom. She would spend the next decade believing someone else committed an unspeakable crime which saw four members of her family killed. Years later, police reappeared, to advise they had confirmed the killer - and Jessi realised with horror that not only she had known him all along, but that her sleepover was the only reason she was still alive

programme.desc: Sara Davies hosts the quiz in which contestants are given their prize money before they have answered a question, and must race against time to keep hold of it. programme.desc: Ant and Dec welcome back brothers Alistair and Ashley to finish their game, determined to do their family proud, before Denise and her nephew Pablo get their chance to take on the Limitless Ladder. A pillar of his community, Pablo has two children he wants to leave a legacy for, while Denise has her own wish for family and friends - but Ant & Dec have a surprise for them

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

With DeForest Kelley and guest star Joan Collins

However, the henchman has other plans, setting into motion a violent chain of blackmail, double-crosses and revenge. Action thriller, starring Steven Seagal, Pim Bubear and John Edward Lee

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics help a 15-year-old girl trapped in the back of a crashed car, and a chemical attack at a shopping centre leaves a woman with a burning throat and struggling to breathe

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cyclist Stephen, who's 48, has severely damaged his ankle after coming off his bike deep in woodland in Surrey, and doctors treat a man rushed in after collapsing with a suspected stroke in his garden. Meanwhile, Bridget, who's 70, broke her wrist when she slipped on a mountain path in Greece

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Gary, 50, is brought into St George's hospital by ambulance after a garage collapsed and fell on his legs. His wife Peta joins him, while Maggie, 83, comes in to A&E with suspected sepsis. She's joined by her son and daughter, who also deal with the fact their mother has lung cancer. Plus, cameras follow one-year-old George, who is rushed in with his mother after drinking nail varnish remover

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The Food Unwrapped team explore the snacks aisle. Kate Quilton visits Kenya to find out how many mangoes are in a snack pack, Helen Lawal learns the mystery of the fluffy centre of a snowball, John Whaite cracks open the secret of caramel popcorn, and Andi Oliver investigates what a supermarket aisle means when you're shopping online

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more gems hidden among unwanted possessions, trying to make a profit from church organ pipes and old leather sports car seats

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole calls in a locksmith to bring a safe back from the scrapheap, and a ship's wheel is left in splinters when the presenter falls out with restorer Guy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Brighton Surf hotel, where Jeremy and Elena Mann hope their hands-off hosting style will win the approval of their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Number 16, in Greenwich, London, owned by actor and antiques dealer, Robert Gray. Alas, Jan and Les find the dusty decor off-putting

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week comes from the Fleur de Lis in Sandwich, Kent, with hosts Jan and Les Fisher convinced their traditional place will impress the others

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to Dale End House near Matlock in the Peak District, where owner Sarah Summers is dreading the arrival of Robert after their confrontation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day sees the B&B owners meet for the last time, with the Brighton Surf Hotel's Jeremy and Elena Mann wanting to defend their no-breakfast remote-hosting concept

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dairy farmer's wife and Disney enthusiast Charlene Fry-Henderson hosts the first dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, where she hopes that her locally reared meat, home cooking, and entertainment involving a plastic cow will secure her the £1,000 prize. Her guests are local radio presenter Tommy Jardine, banker Olive Melville, prison custody officer Tammy Rice and trainee teacher Josh Jones

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast and banker Olive Melville hosts the second dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, where she hopes to impress her guests with a crab starter and mousse dessert - but things take a turn for the worse when a conversation about the royal family divides the diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Local radio DJ Tommy Jardine hosts the third dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, and prepares chicken stuffed with haggis - but first he must continue a family tradition and rustle up a sheep for his entertainment. Tommy then tries to impress his guests with a touch of celebrity name-dropping, but young dance DJ and trainee teacher Josh trumps him at his own game

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee teacher and club DJ Josh Jones hosts the fourth night in Dumfries and Galloway, and hopes he can broaden his fellow contestants' palates with his menu of Indian food. He plans lamb pasanda and tandoori monkfish, but Tammy Rice finds something in her glass that wasn't on the menu. However, Josh hopes his disco entertainment might impress the guests enough to bag him the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tammy Rice hosts the final evening in Dumfries and Galloway, hoping to win the £1,000 prize with her no-nonsense approach and traditional Scottish fare. The dinner-party novice rustles up chorizo and cous cous stuffed peppers for her starter and incorporates haggis into her chicken-based main course

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer search Glasgow with two couples whose budgets will not stretch to what they want. Kirstie is with Laura and Euan, who have recently returned to their native Glasgow from Sydney. But they are torn between their desire to be close to the vibrant West End or further out, where their budget will buy them something bigger. Phil has a wide brief with Miriam and David, who have spent eight months searching for their new home. He soon has success - the only problem is, they have each fallen for a different house

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help Ruth and Ken from Caterham, Surrey settle their debate over their three-storey town house. Since their children have flown the nest, the home isn't right for them anymore and Ruth is ready to sell but avid DIYer Ken wants to stay and thinks with some touch ups their home could still be the place for them

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin travels the country to uncover the stories behind the buildings Britain has chosen to protect at the very highest level. In the first episode, he raises Tower Bridge and descends into its Victorian engine rooms, and enters a church beneath which 1500 years of London's history lie buried. In the oldest continuously working theatre in the English-speaking world, Kevin tries out a piece of stage machinery that's still operating after more than two centuries

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary following patients being treated at St George's in south-west London reaches its 200th edition. Ky, 18, is brought in after being knocked off her motorbike in a hit-and-run, and her mum Sam is by her side as doctors assess the damage to her legs and pelvis. Aleks, 41, is suffering from confusion and memory loss after falling from her loft onto her head, and as her husband Paulo arrives at the hospital, medical staff are still unsure as to whether she has sustained permanent brain damage

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 72-year-old is airlifted to St George's after being trampled by a herd of cows while walking her dog, and 13-year-old Tamzen has broken her leg at school trampoline practice. Meanwhile, 18-year-old art student Hermione has cut her hand on a glass at her part-time waitressing job

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin travels the country to uncover the stories behind the buildings Britain has chosen to protect at the very highest level. In the first episode, he raises Tower Bridge and descends into its Victorian engine rooms, and enters a church beneath which 1500 years of London's history lie buried. In the oldest continuously working theatre in the English-speaking world, Kevin tries out a piece of stage machinery that's still operating after more than two centuries

Plus, four-year-old Elliott has come to A&E with his mum after getting a foreign object stuck up his nose

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: In search of the secret to the bubbles in bubble tea, Kate Quilton visits a cassava farm in Kenya to get to the root of it. Jimmy Doherty is in Somerset exploring why sour milk is sneered at, though soured cream is revered. Plus, Helen Lawal reports from Portugal on canned fish

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo travel the length of Britain seeking items to fix up, flog on and sell for profit. Max upcycles a brass propeller into a stylish table lamp, while Henry goes all Victorian with an old pond yacht

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: No-nonsense bikers Wayne and Tracy Dunn arefirst to host at Llwyn Onn Guest House in Betws-y-Coed, north Wales. Former airline cabin crew assistant Nicola Ellershaw makes a less than sparkly discovery when she checks in. Wayne and Tracy challenge their guests to design biker leathers for their own gang, before Jon and Chelli Bean make a shock revelation about Jon's past. At breakfast, Gary Pesticcio challenges his hosts, which his competitors label as game playing, and at feedback time Wayne and Tracy have to face some home truths

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second hosts of the week are newlywed hoteliers Jon and Chelli Bean from the Parkwood Hotel in Stockton-on-Tees

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third hosts of the week are Nicola Ellershaw and Mackenzie Griffey, who hope to impress with the stylish fare on offer at The Pug and Greyhound in Leicestershire

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests' final stay is at The Solar Strand Hotel in Crosskeys, Wales, where Gary Pesticcio and his daughter Naomi hope their hi-tech approach to hospitality will impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid, and the atmosphere turns sour when Jon and Chelli Bean disagree with Gary and Naomi Pesticcio's thoughts on their facility. Plus, Nicola Ellershaw and Mackenzie Griffey of The Pug and Greyhound in Great Glen want to unlock the reasons why their rooms were described as being 'like prison cells'

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Divorce lawyer Michelle Camacho hosts the first dinner party in south London, where she hopes that her 1970s menu of duck a l'orange and coleslaw, vegetables and a Bloody Mary sorbet for dessert will help to secure her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pub manager and artist Matt Wren hosts the second evening in south London, but his dinner party is beset by disasters in the kitchen and arguing guests at the dinner table when Michelle and Licia have a spat. However, one of the five takes a liking to another of the contestants

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Licia Snow hosts the third party in south London, and hopes to impress her guests with rustic and homely touches during her laid-back evening. However, her relaxed and calm approach could be threatened when she clashes with Michelle at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Raphael Steele hosts the fourth night in south London and hopes that his first-ever dinner party will be a success. He plans a Caribbean evening based on old family recipes featuring ackee, saltfish and curried goat, and organises steel drum music for entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Swedish businesswoman Charlotte Sundberg hosts the final party in south London. Her tactics are to present a night full of schnapps, folk songs and classic cuisine from her Scandinavian homeland, which she hopes will help her scoop the cash prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go house-hunting on the Kent coast - Phil's home turf. He is with Ellen, who is moving out from her parents' in Hertfordshire and wants to live in the midst of Margate's vibrant LGBTQ+ community. Meanwhile, Kirstie helps young parents Sophie and Matt, who need to get out of Sophie's mum's and find their own family home in Broadstairs

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer head to Strathaven, Lanarkshire to help Frank and Wilma decide whether to sell their four-bedroom family home or invest in it to make it work for them. Frank is firmly on Team Love It, having raised their two children there, he is convinced they still have many happy years to come in the house, while Wilma is ready to sell now that their kids have flown the nest. Kirstie grapples with a poorly placed conservatory and a kitchen diner that struggles to fit the extend ed family in as she creates a plan to renovate while Phil is put through his paces trying to convince Frank to swap suburban life for sea views

programme.title: Great British Train Journeys from Above
programme.desc: Hugh Bonneville narrates the most iconic steam train journeys in Britain. The first episode tracks the Jacobite Railway, which runs through the Scottish Highlands from Fort William to Mallaig. The programme visits the site of Bonnie Prince Charlie's hideout, and explores the history of the Jacobite Rebellion

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Zach, who's 25, is rushed to A&E after completely severing four fingers in a building site accident. Staff and plastic surgeons assess the damage and stem the bloody wound. Cameras also follow former teacher Ricky, who's 85. She has hurt her back after falling at home, and 10-year-old Daquon was hit by a motorbike when he was on his way to school. He has memory loss and a badly broken arm

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An episode focusing on the lengths the staff go to in the line of duty, with patients including 23-year-old Joe, who is rushed to St George's after his cousin accidentally stabbed him in the thigh. Psychiatric nurse Christie, 25, arrives in resus with severe burns after being attacked by a patient, and two-year-old Freddie won't let doctors look in his eye after complaining of pain

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A delivery driver is the victim of a carjacking and ends up being dragged along the road. On the M40, 13 stowaway migrants are found in a lorry full of tyres and in need of urgent medical care. A man collapses while having a fit in the middle of a busy road, and a woman experiences uncontrollable spasms in her legs at the stress of losing a child

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 72-year-old is airlifted to St George's after being trampled by a herd of cows while walking her dog, and 13-year-old Tamzen has broken her leg at school trampoline practice. Meanwhile, 18-year-old art student Hermione has cut her hand on a glass at her part-time waitressing job

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits a farm in Cornwall to get to the bottom of the 'flower' element in a in cauliflower. Helen Lawal is in Spain to find out what makes lemon juice last so long, while Matt Tebbutt gets the beers in at a Worksop brewery, as he explores what the term 'bottle conditioned' actually means

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Leeds and Preston in search of people's junk and clutter to upchucked into valuable new items. Henry makes an international set of clocks out of dashboard time pieces, while Simon builds an eccentric chandelier from car headlights

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and Wirral, where items include a 1920s dancehall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin travels the country to uncover the stories behind the buildings Britain has chosen to protect at the very highest level. In the first episode, he raises Tower Bridge and descends into its Victorian engine rooms, and enters a church beneath which 1500 years of London's history lie buried. In the oldest continuously working theatre in the English-speaking world, Kevin tries out a piece of stage machinery that's still operating after more than two centuries

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cyclist Stephen, who's 48, has severely damaged his ankle after coming off his bike deep in woodland in Surrey, and doctors treat a man rushed in after collapsing with a suspected stroke in his garden. Meanwhile, Bridget, who's 70, broke her wrist when she slipped on a mountain path in Greece

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Gary, 50, is brought into St George's hospital by ambulance after a garage collapsed and fell on his legs. His wife Peta joins him, while Maggie, 83, comes in to A&E with suspected sepsis. She's joined by her son and daughter, who also deal with the fact their mother has lung cancer. Plus, cameras follow one-year-old George, who is rushed in with his mother after drinking nail varnish remover

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The Food Unwrapped team explore the snacks aisle. Kate Quilton visits Kenya to find out how many mangoes are in a snack pack, Helen Lawal learns the mystery of the fluffy centre of a snowball, John Whaite cracks open the secret of caramel popcorn, and Andi Oliver investigates what a supermarket aisle means when you're shopping online

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more gems hidden among unwanted possessions, trying to make a profit from church organ pipes and old leather sports car seats

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole calls in a locksmith to bring a safe back from the scrapheap, and a ship's wheel is left in splinters when the presenter falls out with restorer Guy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Brighton Surf hotel, where Jeremy and Elena Mann hope their hands-off hosting style will win the approval of their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Number 16, in Greenwich, London, owned by actor and antiques dealer, Robert Gray. Alas, Jan and Les find the dusty decor off-putting

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week comes from the Fleur de Lis in Sandwich, Kent, with hosts Jan and Les Fisher convinced their traditional place will impress the others

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to Dale End House near Matlock in the Peak District, where owner Sarah Summers is dreading the arrival of Robert after their confrontation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day sees the B&B owners meet for the last time, with the Brighton Surf Hotel's Jeremy and Elena Mann wanting to defend their no-breakfast remote-hosting concept

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dairy farmer's wife and Disney enthusiast Charlene Fry-Henderson hosts the first dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, where she hopes that her locally reared meat, home cooking, and entertainment involving a plastic cow will secure her the £1,000 prize. Her guests are local radio presenter Tommy Jardine, banker Olive Melville, prison custody officer Tammy Rice and trainee teacher Josh Jones

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast and banker Olive Melville hosts the second dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, where she hopes to impress her guests with a crab starter and mousse dessert - but things take a turn for the worse when a conversation about the royal family divides the diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Local radio DJ Tommy Jardine hosts the third dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, and prepares chicken stuffed with haggis - but first he must continue a family tradition and rustle up a sheep for his entertainment. Tommy then tries to impress his guests with a touch of celebrity name-dropping, but young dance DJ and trainee teacher Josh trumps him at his own game

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee teacher and club DJ Josh Jones hosts the fourth night in Dumfries and Galloway, and hopes he can broaden his fellow contestants' palates with his menu of Indian food. He plans lamb pasanda and tandoori monkfish, but Tammy Rice finds something in her glass that wasn't on the menu. However, Josh hopes his disco entertainment might impress the guests enough to bag him the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tammy Rice hosts the final evening in Dumfries and Galloway, hoping to win the £1,000 prize with her no-nonsense approach and traditional Scottish fare. The dinner-party novice rustles up chorizo and cous cous stuffed peppers for her starter and incorporates haggis into her chicken-based main course

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer search Glasgow with two couples whose budgets will not stretch to what they want. Kirstie is with Laura and Euan, who have recently returned to their native Glasgow from Sydney. But they are torn between their desire to be close to the vibrant West End or further out, where their budget will buy them something bigger. Phil has a wide brief with Miriam and David, who have spent eight months searching for their new home. He soon has success - the only problem is, they have each fallen for a different house

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help Ruth and Ken from Caterham, Surrey settle their debate over their three-storey town house. Since their children have flown the nest, the home isn't right for them anymore and Ruth is ready to sell but avid DIYer Ken wants to stay and thinks with some touch ups their home could still be the place for them

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary following patients being treated at St George's in south-west London reaches its 200th edition. Aleks, 41, is suffering from confusion and memory loss after falling from her loft onto her head, and as her husband Paulo arrives at the hospital, medical staff are still unsure as to whether she has sustained permanent brain damage

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo travel the length of Britain seeking items to fix up, flog on and sell for profit. Max upcycles a brass propeller into a stylish table lamp, while Henry goes all Victorian with an old pond yacht

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: No-nonsense bikers Wayne and Tracy Dunn arefirst to host at Llwyn Onn Guest House in Betws-y-Coed, north Wales. Former airline cabin crew assistant Nicola Ellershaw makes a less than sparkly discovery when she checks in. Wayne and Tracy challenge their guests to design biker leathers for their own gang, before Jon and Chelli Bean make a shock revelation about Jon's past. At breakfast, Gary Pesticcio challenges his hosts, which his competitors label as game playing, and at feedback time Wayne and Tracy have to face some home truths

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second hosts of the week are newlywed hoteliers Jon and Chelli Bean from the Parkwood Hotel in Stockton-on-Tees

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third hosts of the week are Nicola Ellershaw and Mackenzie Griffey, who hope to impress with the stylish fare on offer at The Pug and Greyhound in Leicestershire

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests' final stay is at The Solar Strand Hotel in Crosskeys, Wales, where Gary Pesticcio and his daughter Naomi hope their hi-tech approach to hospitality will impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Divorce lawyer Michelle Camacho hosts the first dinner party in south London, where she hopes that her 1970s menu of duck a l'orange and coleslaw, vegetables and a Bloody Mary sorbet for dessert will help to secure her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pub manager and artist Matt Wren hosts the second evening in south London, but his dinner party is beset by disasters in the kitchen and arguing guests at the dinner table when Michelle and Licia have a spat. However, one of the five takes a liking to another of the contestants

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Licia Snow hosts the third party in south London, and hopes to impress her guests with rustic and homely touches during her laid-back evening. However, her relaxed and calm approach could be threatened when she clashes with Michelle at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Raphael Steele hosts the fourth night in south London and hopes that his first-ever dinner party will be a success. He plans a Caribbean evening based on old family recipes featuring ackee, saltfish and curried goat, and organises steel drum music for entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Swedish businesswoman Charlotte Sundberg hosts the final party in south London. Her tactics are to present a night full of schnapps, folk songs and classic cuisine from her Scandinavian homeland, which she hopes will help her scoop the cash prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go house-hunting on the Kent coast - Phil's home turf. Meanwhile, Kirstie helps young parents Sophie and Matt, who need to get out of Sophie's mum's and find their own family home in Broadstairs

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer head to Strathaven, Lanarkshire to help Frank and Wilma decide whether to sell their four-bedroom family home or invest in it to make it work for them. Frank is firmly on Team Love It, having raised their two children there, he is convinced they still have many happy years to come in the house, while Wilma is ready to sell now that their kids have flown the nest. Kirstie grapples with a poorly placed conservatory and a kitchen diner that struggles to fit the extend ed family in as she creates a plan to renovate while Phil is put through his paces trying to convince Frank to swap suburban life for sea views

programme.title: Nixon in the Den
programme.desc: David Reynolds examines the controversial career of former US President Richard Nixon, using audio recordings and rare home movie footage to reveal his obsessive secrecy and mistrust of key aides. programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Palaces
programme.desc: An exploration of the history of Kensington Palace, which is currently the official London residence of the Duke and Duchess of Gloucester, the Duke and Duchess of Kent, and Prince and Princess Michael of Kent. Kate Williams also tells the tale of Elizabeth I's make-up, which was so poisonous that it would be illegal today, with her foundation made of lead and vinegar, her rouge from lead carbonate, and her lipstick from ground alabaster, with side-effects including digestive problems and respiratory issues

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the Normans' expansion toward southern Europe and the Middle East in the 11th century. Southern Italy and Sicily were conquered and a prosperous kingdom flourished on the island, which became an important centre of medieval culture and an example of harmony between religions. However, Pope Urban II's call for holy war against the Muslims, to win Jerusalem back, led to the Normans embarking on the First Crusade

programme.title: Nixon in the Den
programme.desc: David Reynolds examines the controversial career of former US President Richard Nixon, using audio recordings and rare home movie footage to reveal his obsessive secrecy and mistrust of key aides. Arguing that he was genuinely successful as an international statesman, the historian considers how the methods that won him acclaim also doomed his presidency in the Watergate scandal

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: The use of propaganda in the Nazi regime. Hitler's fascination with propaganda was born in the trenches of the First World War. There he saw it emerge as a potent tool of psychological warfare, one that he would harness to build the Nazi movement over the course of the 1920s and early 1930s

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Palaces
programme.desc: A look at the lost palace of Whitehall, which was destroyed by fire in 1698, with the Banqueting House the sole surviving building from the complex. Plus, the story of a couple from Manchester who decided to invite the Queen to their wedding in 2012, while Kate Williams explores the narcissistic motivations behind the Field of the Cloth of Gold, one of the biggest Tudor parties in history

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Historian Stephen Baxter explores his own theory that the Domesday Book was commissioned to confer revolutionary powers on the monarchy in the 11th century, and not intended as a means to collect taxes. He examines how the first great national survey, which recorded who owned every piece of land and property in England, was a massive logistical exercise that documented the traumatic impact of the Norman Conquest

programme.desc: Bill is worried by Liza's radio interview and Hannah faints at the wine bar, fuelling speculation that she might be pregnant - but Faith is stunned by the truth. Comedy, with James Bolam, Lynda Bellingham and Belinda Lang, first aired in 1992

programme.title: Drop the Dead Donkey
programme.desc: A new secretary reveals embarrassing truths about Joy's past, while Gus prepares for a date by doing a practice-run with Sally - who suspects he may be in love with her

programme.title: Drop the Dead Donkey
programme.desc: Helen invites some of her colleagues to a dinner party to meet her new girlfriend, but the guests prove to be troublesome. Gus finds his date has become obsessed with him

programme.title: Vampire Hookers (1978)
programme.desc: An aged vampire uses seductive vampiric women posing as sex workers to lure men to his lair. When their commander vanishes, two Navy sailors fall into the deadly trap. Comedy horror, starring John Carradine

programme.title: Brimstone and Treacle (1982)
programme.desc: Asinister stranger worms his way into a middle-aged couple's home by offering to take care of their comatose daughter - but it is only once he's part of the family that his true nature shines through. Drama based on Dennis Potter's TV play, starring Sting, Denholm Elliott, Joan Plowright and Suzanna Hamilton

programme.desc: The Coaching Inn has an unwelcome guest - Harry's ex-wife Alison, who is later kidnapped. Drama, starring Michael Elphick and David Daker

programme.title: The Fenn Street Gang
programme.desc: When Dennis is persuaded to holiday by the sea, his father comes along for the cure. To the horror of elderly hotel residents, the sea air revives both father and son.

programme.title: Bubblegum & Cheese
programme.desc: Alex Dyke presents an hour of bubblegum pop and cheesy tracks to make you feel good

programme.title: The Gaffer

programme.title: Hallelujah

programme.title: Rewind Time Machine
programme.desc: The eighth round of the AA-Westward Television Grand Prix British Stock Car racing series in 1976.

programme.title: Second Thoughts
programme.desc: Joe vows to marry Louise, leaving Faith flabbergasted at how respectable her teenage son has become - but she's soon distracted by an unexpected development

programme.desc: Shelly's husband Jason is delighted to be assigned to the case, with no sense where the trail may lead. While he and his colleague Fiona focus on a sex worker, Shelly realises she may have left evidence in the dead man's hotel room. Crime thriller, starring Charlene McKenna
