programme.title: Donal's Real Time Recipes
programme.desc: Donal Skehan demonstrates recipes all about speed, simplicity, and the power of prep. The chef cooks a fish cake paired with summer noodles, a kebab plate with Shirazi salad, and a Greek salad a glow-up with whipped feta

programme.title: How to Cook Well in Morocco
programme.desc: Rory O'Connell continues his exploration of Marrakesh, learning how a traditional pigeon pastry pie, called a pastilla, is made at L'Hotel Marrakech. The chef also visits Anima Gardens, an exotic garden filled with sculptures and ceramics, before heading to Al Fassia restaurant, where all the staff are women. At home in his East Cork kitchen, Rory makes a Moroccan orange and almond cake

programme.title: RTÉ News: Nine O'Clock and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Uncharted with Ray Goggins
programme.desc: Comedian PJ Gallagher and actor Liam Cunningham along with Ray Goggins take to the high altitude dirt roads of the Himalayas on motorbike. Venturing into the dizzying heights of Upper Mustang, they'll eat, pray and bike their way along some of the diciest roads in the world, a real test of both nerve and skill for all three bikers

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: Crime drama following Chief Inspector Maigret - a rising star in the Paris police. Maigret investigates a series of bank robberies. He turns his attention to the murder of a burglar, Honoré Cuendet - until the robbers strike again. Starring Benjamin Wainwright

programme.title: Red Eye
programme.desc: During the embassy evacuation, Brody breaks loose and helps Hana seize Fox and recover the detonator, but he escapes in a clash with security. Hana and Brody retrieve stolen evidence and flee to Delaney. Meanwhile, the conspiracy's mastermind is exposed in flashbacks

programme.title: Any Given Day: Cork University Hospital
programme.desc: Sean's family rush him to the CUH emergency department as he is suffering severe chest pains and has a history of heart attacks. William has a brain aneurysm that is in danger of rupturing, putting him at serious risk of a stroke. Another Sean has cancer and his tumour is causing him significant blockage in his bowel. He requires a stoma bag to be fitted

programme.title: Duffy's Pub
programme.desc: US actor Patrick Duffy, a third-generation Irishman, has always been fascinated by the communities that develop around their local pub, so he travels to Ireland to see for himself how bars have evolved and changed. Along with his partner, the actress Linda Purl, Patrick visits many Duffy's Pubs and other legendary boozers across Dublin, Galway, Donegal, Mayo and Tipperary, to explore pub culture and the changes it has undergone over the years. He ends his journey in his ancestral home of Kilmovee, Mayo, where his grandfather, also Patrick, lived before leaving for a new life in Montana

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: In the north Dublin suburb of Harmonstown, Dermot Bannon helps Eimear and Adam Fox by tackling the issue of space in their three-bedroom, mid-terrace former corporation house. Their home is short on storage, missing an upstairs bathroom and lacking any real sense of flow. With clever pods, a DIY-minded plumber and one costly miscalculation, the project soon proves that even the smartest plans can wobble

programme.title: High Road, Low Road
programme.desc: Travel show pairing up celebrities and sending them off to far-flung destinations for a light-hearted adventure of discovery, culture and fun. But while one enjoys a luxury break, the other is forced to travel economy all the way, including cheap hotels and food

programme.title: Cheap Irish Homes
programme.desc: In the series finale, Maggie Molloy and Kieran McCarthy travel to Mayo to meet hairdresser Patrice Lyttle, who is desperate to get on the property ladder, close to where she works in Westport

programme.title: Home Rescue: The Big Fix
programme.desc: The team help to declutter Margaret Pierce and her son Shane's fourth-floor apartment in Scholarstown, Co Dublin that is overwhelmed with clutter. Self-confessed bargain hunter Margaret knows she has a problem and needs to clear out but a chronic health condition has made that impossible for her. Designer Dee and builder Pete rethink the limited space creating clever storage solutions and a future-proof design to give the family the fresh start they need

programme.title: Le Chéile
programme.desc: The story of East Belfast GAA during its first full year of play, revealing how it began with a tweet in May 2020 inviting people from all backgrounds, beliefs and abilities to join. The first members explain how the club was built from scratch, unlike most GAA clubs, which have been at the heart of parishes and families for generations. In Irish

Over the years of ocean exploration, he works out where the fertile areas of the ocean are, and his journeys bring him into contact with some of the largest creatures ever to have lived. The story takes a dark turn as Ken discovers thousands of dolphins are being killed every year in the Northeast Atlantic, many of them in Irish waters

He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the city's biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. programme.desc: Wolcott continues to make enemies for himself in the town, leading the camp's leaders to call a meeting after a public brawl with Utter. Meanwhile, Swearengen has disturbing news for Alma Garrett - but she is unsure whether she can trust him. Drama, starring Garret Dillahunt, Molly Parker and Ian McShane

programme.title: The Krays (1990)
programme.desc: Biopic of the infamous twin brothers who built up a criminal empire in the East End of London during the 1960s. Seizing territory from an already established rival by force, they quickly rose to prominence in the city's gangland culture. However, over time the brothers began to clash, while enemies stood ready to bring them down. Gangster biopic, starring Gary and Martin Kemp, Billie Whitelaw and Tom Bell.

programme.title: Sudden Death (1995)
programme.desc: Terrorists seize control of an ice-hockey stadium in the middle of a match attended by the US vice president. Taking the politician hostage, they threaten to blow up the building at the final whistle - but a resourceful former fireman takes it upon himself to thwart their dastardly plot. Action thriller, starring Jean-Claude Van Damme, Powers Boothe, Raymond J Barry and Whittni Wright

programme.title: The Lawnmower Man (1992)
programme.desc: A scientist's ill-advised experiments with virtual reality equipment transform a mentally handicapped gardener into a computer-enhanced megalomaniac with the power to make his dreams of world domination come true. Sci-fi thriller, starring Pierce Brosnan, Jeff Fahey, Jenny Wright, Geoffrey Lewis and Austin O'Brien

programme.title: Paulie (1998)
programme.desc: An intelligent parrot falls into late-night conversation with a janitor and ends up recounting the tale of his epic cross-country journey in search of a new life - a plan that went awry when an evil scientist managed to get his hands on the brilliant bird for his own nefarious ends. Family adventure, starring Gena Rowlands, Tony Shalhoub, Cheech Marin, Bruce Davison and the voice of Jay Mohr

programme.title: The Cable Guy (1996)
programme.desc: A man makes the mistake of engaging a cable TV installer in conversation in a ploy to get all the channels for free. However, the manic repairman shows an invincible determination to be his new best friend - and does not take kindly to rejection. Comedy directed by Ben Stiller, starring Jim Carrey, Matthew Broderick and George Segal

programme.title: Liar Liar (1997)
programme.desc: A lawyer whose underhand ploys to win cases leave him little time to see his son discovers he is incapable of lying after the boy's birthday wish makes his dad tell only the truth. However, this causes a host of problems as the attorney tries to muddle his way through a high-profile divorce settlement. Comedy, with Jim Carrey, Maura Tierney, Justin Cooper, Jennifer Tilly and Cary Elwes

programme.title: Ace Ventura: Pet Detective (1994)
programme.desc: An eccentric but brilliant former cop sets himself up to champion the underdog - as a private eye specialising in locating missing animals. The zany detective is called in to find a kidnapped dolphin - the mascot of an American football team - and uncovers a plot involving high-level police corruption. Comedy, starring Jim Carrey, Courteney Cox, Sean Young, Tone Loc and Noble Willingham

programme.title: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls (1995)
programme.desc: The eccentric pet detective is coaxed out of self-imposed exile in a Buddhist monastery to visit Africa and help search for a sacred white bat. However, his unique investigative talents are tested to the limits as he faces a race against time to find the missing creature and prevent a tribal war. Comedy sequel, starring Jim Carrey, Simon Callow and Bob Gunton

programme.title: Dumb and Dumber (1994)
programme.desc: Dimwitted Rhode Island limousine driver Lloyd Christmas and his equally vacuous friend Harry Dunne embark on a disaster-fraught cross-country trip to Aspen, where they hope to reunite a briefcase with its female owner. However, the pair are unaware the case contains a $1million ransom they would have been better leaving well alone. Farrelly brothers comedy, starring Jim Carrey, Jeff Daniels, Lauren Holly, Teri Garr and Mike Starr

programme.title: A League of Their Own (1992)
programme.desc: An alcoholic former professional player reluctantly agrees to train an all-female baseball team during the Second World War. With star player Dottie Hinson and her sister Kit on board, they look set to dominate the league - until sibling rivalry rears its head. Comedy drama, starring Geena Davis, Lori Petty, Tom Hanks and Madonna

programme.title: Legends of the Fall (1994)
programme.desc: A retired army colonel has raised his three sons alone on their remote Montana ranch. But the all-male household is thrown into turmoil when the youngest son brings home his beautiful fiancee, causing jealousy and tensions that culminate in tragedy. Edward Zwick's period drama, starring Anthony Hopkins, Brad Pitt, Aidan Quinn, Julia Ormond and Henry Thomas

programme.title: The Shawshank Redemption (1994)
programme.desc: A banker is wrongly jailed for his wife's murder and over the course of a life sentence he forms a close friendship with a fellow inmate. He also earns a position of trust as book-keeper to the corrupt prison governor - but makes an enemy of his benefactor as he tries to clear his name. Frank Darabont's drama, starring Tim Robbins, Morgan Freeman, Bob Gunton, William Sadler, Clancy Brown and Gil Bellows

programme.title: Falling Down (1993)
programme.desc: A stressed motorist cracks under the pressures of everyday life, and the day that started out with a simple journey to visit his estranged family turns into an increasingly violent trek across Los Angeles. Director Joel Schumacher's satirical urban thriller, starring Michael Douglas, Robert Duvall, Barbara Hershey, Rachel Ticotin and Frederic Forrest

programme.title: Lethal Weapon 4 (1998)
programme.desc: Mismatched LAPD detectives Riggs and Murtaugh stumble across a ship transporting Chinese immigrants into the US. As they try to get to the bottom of the organisation behind the smuggling, they find themselves up against a master criminal who is an expert in martial arts and heads a triad responsible for people-trafficking and forgery. Action adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Rene Russo, Joe Pesci, Jet Li and Chris Rock

programme.title: The Lawnmower Man (1992)
programme.desc: A scientist's ill-advised experiments with virtual reality equipment transform a mentally handicapped gardener into a computer-enhanced megalomaniac with the power to make his dreams of world domination come true. Sci-fi thriller, starring Pierce Brosnan, Jeff Fahey, Jenny Wright, Geoffrey Lewis and Austin O'Brien

programme.title: Breakdown (1997)
programme.desc: A stranded motorist is desperate when his wife disappears without a trace on a road trip - and the reluctance of the locals to help convinces him something sinister is going on. When it turns out that she has been abducted by a crazed trucker and his cronies, he is determined to set her free and make sure the villains pay for their crime. Thriller, starring Kurt Russell, Kathleen Quinlan, JT Walsh, MC Gainey, Jack Noseworthy and Rex Linn

programme.title: First Knight (1995)
programme.desc: A wandering swordsman is knighted for his fighting prowess at the court of King Arthur and his wife, Lady Guinevere. However, the new knight and the queen develop a dangerous and secret attraction for each other, while a neighbouring warlord threatens the downfall of the kingdom. Adventure, starring Richard Gere, Sean Connery, Julia Ormond and Ben Cross

Frank Darabont's drama, starring Tim Robbins, Morgan Freeman, Bob Gunton, William Sadler, Clancy Brown and Gil Bellows

programme.title: Boyz N the Hood (1991)
programme.desc: A bright but underachieving teenager with a domineering single father strikes up a friendship with two brothers, and over time, the three young men each follow their own path - but a tragedy eventually leaves them facing a dangerous choice. Drama, starring Cuba Gooding Jr, Laurence Fishburne, Morris Chestnut and Ice Cube

programme.title: Men in Black (1997)
programme.desc: A streetwise cop is enlisted by a top-secret government force dedicated to policing alien visitors to Earth, teaming up with a veteran agent just as a body-swapping extraterrestrial giant insect begins a reign of terror throughout New York City. Sci-fi comedy, starring Will Smith, Tommy Lee Jones, Linda Fiorentino, Rip Torn and Vincent D'Onofrio

programme.title: Bad Boys (1995)
programme.desc: Detectives Mike Lowrey and Marcus Burnett search for a $100million heroin consignment brazenly stolen by a French drug kingpin from the police evidence room. However, after a mix-up over a phone call from a potential witness, each cop has to assume the other's identity - Lowrey becoming the henpecked family man and Burnett the playboy. Michael Bay's action thriller, starring Will Smith, Martin Lawrence, Tea Leoni, Tcheky Karyo and Theresa Randle

programme.title: Lethal Weapon 3 (1992)
programme.desc: As Murtagh counts down his last few days on the force before retirement, he and Riggs go on the trail of a former LAPD officer who is using his insider knowledge to steal confiscated weaponry and ammunition, then selling it on to criminal gang bosses. The duo are assigned to work with internal affairs investigator Lorna Cole, while they also get help from old friend - and reformed small-time crook - Leo Getz. Action thriller sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Joe Pesci and Rene Russo

programme.title: Demolition Man (1993)
programme.desc: Maverick cop John Spartan is frozen alive in 1996 and held in cryonic suspension for a crime he did not commit. Decades into the future, the dictator of a seemingly utopian city unleashes a criminal psychopath against his political enemy - but he proves impossible to control, forcing the authorities to revive Spartan to defeat him. Sci-fi thriller, with Sylvester Stallone, Wesley Snipes, Sandra Bullock and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.desc: A couple are reunited with a man they grew up with in a children's home years earlier - but the apparently happy reunion soon dredges up dark secrets from their past, and it's not long before tensions bubble to the surface. Drama, starring Cal Macaninch, Daniel Newman, Richard Mylan and Rachel Fielding

programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey (2025)
programme.desc: New York bachelor David is due to drive to his friend's wedding, but a wheel clamp on his vehicle derails those plans. He acquires a new drive from the mysterious Car Rental Company managed by two nameless agents, who are oddly familiar with his dating history. At the wedding, David meets fellow singleton Sarah and squanders an opportunity to dance with her. On the way home, the rental car's GPS asks him if he would like to embark on a big bold beautiful journey. When David agrees, the virtual assistant guides him to Sarah and they follow a winding route between physical doors that open into episodes from their respective pasts. Romantic drama, starring Colin Farrell and Margot Robbie

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw 'acquires' an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennell's drama based on Emily Bronte's romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Eddington (2025)
programme.desc: A stand-off between a small-town sheriff and mayor sparks a powder keg as neighbours are pitted against each other in Eddington, New Mexico. Modern-day Western, starring Joaquin Phoenix, Emma Stone and Pedro Pascal

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Dear Ms: A Revolution in Print
programme.desc: When groundbreaking, feminist Ms magazine was founded in 1971, it soon changed the media landscape by bringing women's concerns directly into the mainstream. Though the publication faced some initial misogynist pushback, it quickly caught on and became one of the decade's success stories. This documentary analyses, the early years of the then-monthly - now quarterly - periodical, exploring the challenges it faced and hurdles it overcame through the lens of three of its most famous covers, featuring archival footage and interviews

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly

programme.desc: Henry Cole and his partners Allen Millyard and Guy Willison are back on the road trawling Britain's sheds and auction houses for rusty relics to restore and sell on for a tidy profit - this time they head down to Dorset to meet Tim, the owner of a delectable collection of trinkets. After coming away with some choice items, they return to the Ledbury auctions for yet more items before heading down the road to the Cotswold Oil Engine and Preservation Society's famous bring and buy sale

programme.title: Field of Vampires
programme.desc: Part one of two. A plot in Poland containing 30 or so apparent 'deviant burials'. One woman was buried with a sickle across her neck and a padlock on her toe - this corpse was believed to be a real-life vampire. An international team of experts uses cutting-edge science and historical research to investigate who this woman was and why she was so feared

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Exploring the case of Ted Kaczynski, known as the Unabomber, a twisted mastermind who used homemade bombs to terrorise America between 1978 and 1995

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Actor Laurence Fishburne investigates the case of the Boston Strangler, who murdered 13 women in Boston, Massachusetts in the early 1960s

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: In Victorville, Ivy takes it easy, letting Ivy Jr run the operation. Kenny feels run down and his lack of focus lands him a locker - though there's a pleasant surprise in the end. Mary, Shana and Edwina just cannot seem to make anything stick, so decide to embrace it, doing what they do best.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Cloudy with a Chance of Profit
programme.desc: It's a momentous occasion in Van Nuys for Darrell, and he enjoys a completely 'groovy time' with it. Ivy starts his day off with an unwelcome bang, while Brandi and Jarrod are left wondering whether their unit will bring clear skies or clouds ahead

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Let's Give 'Em Something to Tonka About
programme.desc: It's a long way to Murietta, and Dave is counting on his competition being unwilling to buy a big locker they need to move. Ivy and Ivy Jr find their day somewhat therapeutic, Mary finally finds a tiny treasure to take home, while Rene stands his ground to protect his honour

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The buyers head to Torrance, where Dave sees his profits grow - amongst other things. Mary finds clothes that don't fit her, which turns out to be a good thing. Plus, Kenny takes a chance at being an early bird, but winds up looking more like a dodo

programme.title: Britain's Lost Battlefields with Rob Bell
programme.desc: The Battle of Hastings of October 1066 ended Anglo-Saxon rule in England and resulted in the transformation of our language and culture. The events of the battle were immortalised in the Bayeux Tapestry, which appears to show King Harold dying with an arrow through his eye. However, Rob learns that the story may not be as simple as that

programme.title: Britain's Lost Battlefields with Rob Bell
programme.desc: Rob Bell investigates the events that led to the Battle of Watling Street, Boudicca's uprising against the Roman occupation of Britain in 60-61 AD

programme.title: Britain's Lost Battlefields with Rob Bell
programme.desc: Rob visits Bosworth Field, site of the decisive battle in 1485 that ended the Wars of the Roses and ushered in the Tudor royal dynasty. He considers the key stages of the battle with historians and battle re-enactors, while Professor Turi King of Leicester University reveals how the remains of Richard III were discovered

programme.title: Dirty Old Cars
programme.desc: In Georgia, a cockroach-infested 1940s Ford goes from disgusting to deluxe. Then, in North Carolina a beat-up BMW transforms into a bright shiny beamer

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick and Corey evaluate a signed Righteous Brothers record. Will they feel 'that lovin' feeling' or will the deal fall flat?

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: What Came First: The Chicken or the Auction?
programme.desc: Overwhelmed by the sheer number of units up for auction in Rialto, Mary and Ivy wager that each can better predict final locker prices than the other. Dave's locker closely resembles a chop shop, while Rene and Tatianna catch a stash of vintage sporting goods

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Emily brings her mother along, Darrell wastes no time finding an advantage, Ivy and Ivy Jr clean up with their locker, while Kenny gets some advice from a music legend. Shana and Edwina settle for thrifting what's in front of them

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Gales batter Desert Hot Springs, and the buyers do their best to keep standing. Rene and Gunther dust off an old locker, Brandi and Jarrod's unit takes a turn and ends up lapping the competition, while Kenny steps up his game big time

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Not All That Glitters Is Gourd
programme.desc: Back in the Californian city of Orange, the Vegas Ladies arrive in vintage style - though not everyone agrees. Casey finds the chutzpah to pick up a good unit. Darrell tries to peddle his wares to Emily, but settles for a decent locker

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: People who make a living from bidding for the contents of abandoned storage units. programme.desc: A computer hacker is released from prison after serving six months for crossing the wrong people, only to walk straight into some masked thugs who bundle him into their car. The Five-0 detectives soon track down the vehicle with the help of the man's autistic little brother - but the news is not good, setting them out on a murder investigation. Meanwhile, Mary arrives in Hawaii and discovers her mother is still alive, so McGarrett stages a reunion - but he doesn't bank on his sister bringing along the motor-mouthed OAP she's been caring for. Christine Lahti, Alan Ruck and Shelley Berman guest star

programme.title: Intergalactic

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: The team members discover an inhabited Earth-like planet named Akaali and disguise themselves to look like its citizens in order to undertake an exploratory expedition. The crew learns the inhabitants are suffering from mysterious plague-like lesions and tries to help them find the cause. Scott Bakula and Jolene Blalock star

programme.title: Ocean's Eleven (2001)
programme.desc: A suave ex-convict assembles a gang of sophisticated crooks to pull off a daring heist. He intends to rob three of Las Vegas's biggest casinos simultaneously - and also win back his estranged wife, who is now dating the ruthless owner of all three gambling establishments. Steven Soderbergh's crime comedy, starring George Clooney, Brad Pitt, Julia Roberts, Matt Damon and Bernie Mac

programme.title: Fast & Furious 7 (2015)
programme.desc: After defeating Owen Shaw and his crew and securing amnesty for their past crimes, Dom, Brian and the rest of the team return home to live normal lives again. However Shaw's older brother Deckard - a rogue special forces assassin vows revenge on Dom, who he holds responsible for leaving Owen in a coma. Action thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Jason Statham and Paul Walker in his last film role

programme.title: Fast & Furious 8 (2017)
programme.desc: Street racing criminal Dominic Toretto is drawn out of retirement by an encounter with a ruthless cyberterrorist, who recruits him for an international criminal scheme. As he sets out to steal the technology she needs to unleash chaos on the world, he is drawn into conflict with both old enemies and his former gang. Action adventure sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Charlize Theron, Dwayne Johnson and Kurt Russell

programme.title: The Cinema List
programme.desc: A look at the some of the most notorious lawbreakers in film, from portrayals of ruthless crime bosses to true stories of thieves at large and smugglers on the run

programme.title: Ocean's Eleven (2001)
programme.desc: A suave ex-convict assembles a gang of sophisticated crooks to pull off a daring heist. He intends to rob three of Las Vegas's biggest casinos simultaneously - and also win back his estranged wife, who is now dating the ruthless owner of all three gambling establishments. Steven Soderbergh's crime comedy, starring George Clooney, Brad Pitt, Julia Roberts, Matt Damon and Bernie Mac

programme.title: Ocean's Twelve (2004)
programme.desc: The crooks reunite when an old enemy catches up with them and demands they repay the money they stole from him three years previously. In response, the gang comes up with another larcenous plan - but reckons without the interventions of a fellow thief and a sultry Interpol agent. Crime caper sequel, starring George Clooney, Brad Pitt, Julia Roberts, Catherine Zeta-Jones, Matt Damon, Andy Garcia and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Ocean's Thirteen (2007)
programme.desc: Danny Ocean rounds up his band of crooks for one last heist. Gang member Reuben has been left traumatised and in a coma after being double-crossed by crooked casino owner Willy Bank, and Danny decides that payback is in order. He plans a scheme that will leave Bank penniless, but needs the help of an old enemy. Comedy crime caper sequel, with George Clooney, Al Pacino, Brad Pitt, Matt Damon, Andy Garcia, Don Cheadle and Elliott Gould

programme.title: Ocean's 8 (2018)
programme.desc: Following in the footsteps of brother Danny, light-fingered Debbie Ocean plans to steal the Toussaint, a $150million necklace from Vogue's Met Gala, with the 'help' of a famous actress. Crime caper, starring Sandra Bullock, Cate Blanchett, Anne Hathaway and Mindy Kaling

programme.title: Marú inár Measc
programme.desc: In February 2013, 36-year-old Olivia Dunlea from Cork was murdered by Darren Murphy, her boyfriend of just eight weeks. Olivia, a mother-of-three and a playschool teacher, was stabbed six times as she lay in her bed in Passage West. Her assailant set fire to the house and fled the scene. With three trials and two appeal hearings, it took Olivia's family seven years to get justice for her murder

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock returns to Fareham, Leicester and Shefford to see some past transformations and find out how her work has affected the lives of the garden owners. In Fareham, Hampshire, Vanessa and Alan have continued to improve on their Mediterranean wonder with olive trees, old stone walls and a giant Scots pine. A family in Leicester explains how their garden renovation has improved their lives and a tiny plot in Shefford has now become a multi-functional garden

programme.title: Mary Millington: Confessions from the David Galaxy Affair (1979)
programme.desc: A seductive astrologer's carefree life is brought crashing to a halt when he is accused of robbery and cannot win over the only person able to provide an alibi. He faces a possibly greater challenge in providing a woman who has never had an orgasm with the satisfaction she has long been deprived of. Sex comedy, starring Alan Lake and Diana Dors

programme.title: Can You Keep it Up for a Week? (1974)
programme.desc: A workshy man's fiancee challenges him to hold down a steady job - a situation that puts him squarely in the path of temptation. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Jeremy Bulloch, Neil Hallett and Jill Damas

programme.title: Celebrity Coach Trip
programme.desc: The coach is in Lisbon, where tour guide Brendan Sheerin drops a vote twist that puts one couple under instant pressure. The group also make Portuguese custard tarts

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh heads to Swindon to surprise Lorraine, a retired RAF servicewoman and devoted charity volunteer. Because of a spinal injury, Lorraine struggles with mobility issues and lives with her full-time support team - Leonbergers Poppy and Doris. Poppy is a retired therapy dog and Doris is Lorraine's assistance dog, and helps with chores such as laundry and fetching household items. The team creates a fabulous low-maintenance garden as a calming retreat for both Lorraine and her canine family

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Art expert Kate Bliss, trader Linda Lambert and upholsterer Anthony Devine scour the antique shops, scrapyards and flea markets of Monmouth in search of items to make them a profit

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Veteran rock band the Flowers of Progress are on the island to record a new album after years of being apart, and with a major sponsorship deal hanging in the balance, tensions are running high. However, their reunion proves to be short-lived when lead singer Stevie Smith is electrocuted in the swimming pool. It seems the victim had a stormy relationship with his brother and fellow band member Jim, while there are rifts between the others and more than one has an axe to grind. But how could any of them have done the deed when they were all in the studio at the time? To add to Humphrey's problems, he is still finding his feet without Camille, although the arrival of new officer JP helps lighten Dwayne's workload. Neil Morrissey and Sally Phillips guest star

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: The team is called to investigate when a man is found stabbed and his toddler daughter is missing. The discovery of a scar on the victim's arm reveals an unusual custom at the dead man's place of work, and the shadowy boss is cast under further suspicion when an altercation with a social worker leads Carrie to suspect the child is being protected from her father's colleagues

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Saint Marie celebrates getting through to the final of the Inter-Island Volleyball Championships, but the party atmosphere is short-lived when one of the players is stabbed through the heart. With such a high-profile murder, DI Goodman and the team are under pressure to solve the crime as soon as they possibly can, while Commissioner Patterson squares up to old enemy Anton Burrage, the governor-elect of the rival volleyball team's island. Joe Absolom (Doc Martin) and Colin McFarlane (The Dark Knight) guest star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Michelle Collins guest stars as a company boss who brings her staff to Saint Marie on an outdoor team-building exercise - although she isn't around for long as she gets bumped off in her shack. Humphrey tries to work out who is responsible - the aggrieved assistant, the long-lost son, the sister whose husband the victim slept with - or someone else. All is going well, until a shock confession brings the investigation to an abrupt halt - and an unexpected phone call from home only adds to the inspector's problems. With Kaye Wragg, Jason Merrells, Sylvestra Le Touzel, Matthew Lewis and Gary Lewis

programme.desc: The celebratory launch of a new luxury resort is cut short when a woman is found electrocuted in her hotel bathtub - with the door locked from the inside. It looks like a simple case of suicide, but with Jack Mooney no longer around to confirm Commissioner Selwyn's suspicions, DI Neville Parker is temporarily flown over from Manchester to close the case. However, he soon realises that island life is not for him, so when evidence of suspicious activity turns up, he is dismayed to realise he will have to extend his stay and investigate the case as murder. Ralf Little joins the cast, with guest appearances by Steve Pemberton and Samantha Bond

programme.desc: The team is called to investigate when a man is found stabbed and his toddler daughter is missing. The discovery of a scar on the victim's arm reveals an unusual custom at the dead man's place of work, and the shadowy boss is cast under further suspicion when an altercation with a social worker leads Carrie to suspect the child is being protected from her father's colleagues

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Saint Marie celebrates getting through to the final of the Inter-Island Volleyball Championships, but the party atmosphere is short-lived when one of the players is stabbed through the heart. With such a high-profile murder, DI Goodman and the team are under pressure to solve the crime as soon as they possibly can, while Commissioner Patterson squares up to old enemy Anton Burrage, the governor-elect of the rival volleyball team's island. Joe Absolom (Doc Martin) and Colin McFarlane (The Dark Knight) guest star

programme.desc: The celebratory launch of a new luxury resort is cut short when a woman is found electrocuted in her hotel bathtub - with the door locked from the inside. It looks like a simple case of suicide, but with Jack Mooney no longer around to confirm Commissioner Selwyn's suspicions, DI Neville Parker is temporarily flown over from Manchester to close the case. However, he soon realises that island life is not for him, so when evidence of suspicious activity turns up, he is dismayed to realise he will have to extend his stay and investigate the case as murder. Ralf Little joins the cast, with guest appearances by Steve Pemberton and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team investigates the shooting of a murder suspect, and there is no shortage of potential culprits, with his angry wife and vengeful brother high on the list. There's just one little puzzle to solve before the cops can even think about whodunit - at the time of the killing, the victim was in police custody, behind three locked doors guarded by Dwayne and JP. programme.desc: Compo is having difficulty coming to terms with being less agile and becomes jealous of local athlete Ernie Burniston when he spots him riding a bike. programme.desc: Fresh DNA tests on an unidentified body found in 1996 reveal a family link to a Home Office fingerprint analyst, who has not seen her brother since she was seven, when he brought her over to the UK from their home country of Albania. programme.desc: Conway is thrilled to meet a pop idol from his youth, but fails to anticipate the trouble heading his way

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Mike comes to the rescue of a child maths prodigy whose drink has been spiked by the jealous boyfriend of a fellow student. Julia asks Nick to compete in a charity wheelchair race and Michelle is furious when Mike is late for a date

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ronnie returns home to find George has organised a Roman-themed surprise for his 40th birthday. Michelle reveals to Ruth her plans to offer a permanent contract to Mike, and Melody is caught in an awkward position when an irate husband objects to his wife receiving breast reduction surgery - insisting she is fine the way she is

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: On a busy night behind the bar, Mickie notices Jodie is becoming breathless and feverish, and tries to persuade her to seek medical help. Tricia and Mark find working together tense, but agree to be as professional as possible, while Diane suffers from pre-wedding jitters and turns to Ric for support. Her fears may be well-founded, as Owen's stag night ends with him kissing Chrissie

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie welcomes two new nurses to the ED, but while Lloyd gets off to a flying start by saving the life of his first patient in resus, a nervous Scarlett seems convinced her skills lie more in flirting with consultants than in caring for people. Meanwhile, teaching assistant Harry (Lee Mead) is involved in an altercation between a senior colleague and a problem student

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack examines the development of castles from early Iron Age forts to secret tunnels used during the Second World War. He visits Hadrian's Wall, where he takes a look at the remains of Roman toilets, while in Warwick a band of medieval knights tests the castle's defences

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack celebrates Britain's houses and palaces, travelling from the grandeur of Hampton Court in west London to a homely terrace in Beamish in Co Durham. He also visits Cragside, Northumberland, one of the first homes to have electric lighting, and helps restore a 15th-century manor in Kent by demonstrating how plaster used to be made

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Documentary drama about the execution of Charles I, beginning by following the events of Christmas 1648. The king was visited at Windsor by the Earl of Denbigh, seeking to broker terms between the imprisoned monarch and an increasingly hardline parliament. When his visit was rejected, new legislation is drawn up to try the king for treason

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Three-part documentary focusing on the monarch's final days. In the second episode, the country prepares for the unprecedented trial of the King. Although the consensus is that the King is a tyrant, only half of the commissioners appointed actually attend the meetings at Westminster Palace, in fear of being indicted as traitors. On the 20th of January 1649, the three day trial begins, and the King and the Lord President John Bradshaw become embroiled in a battle of wills

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Crystal Skulls
programme.desc: Did the ancients possess the knowledge and ability to create, and cut seemingly complex crystal skulls or do they have extra-terrestrial origins?

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: The Great Pyramid at Giza in Egypt is a Wonder of the World - but when was it built, for what purpose and by who?

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Kirsten O'Brien takes a 15-mile walk along the River Tee, exploring the history of Upper Teesdale, meeting some of extraordinary people who call its banks home and taking a wild swim in its white waters. She begins her trek at South Gare near Redcar - a childhood playground for Kirsten and her brother - and then takes in High Force, Middleton and ancient Egglestone Abbey

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul and Suki are in the Peak District on their UK staycation adventure in their trusty motorhome. The first morning finds them in a campsite north of Bakewell, and they debate who should empty the on-board loo. Later, Suki reveals they are swapping four wheels for two after hiring bikes on the nearby Monsal Trail, a disused former railway line

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul and Suki visit Norfolk for the final leg of their UK tour. Their first stop-off is Great Yarmouth's quieter neighbour, Gorleston-on-Sea. With its three miles of award-winning golden sandy beach, it retains its traditional Victorian seaside holiday vibe - including a Punch and Judy show. The duo discover puppetry's satirical roots before doing their own show

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and Suki Webster take to the roads again, beginning by heading to Cornwall, home to crystal-clear waters, picturesque fishing villages and beautiful beaches. Their first destination is the Lizard - the most southerly point on the British mainland - where they go seal watching, before going out with a local fisherman to try their hands at catching spider crabs

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and Suki Webster explore the Cotswolds, helping Richard and his apprentice Innis build a dry stone wall before driving to their first off-grid campsite, pitching up for the night for an al fresco three-course dinner. The next day they visit the the Rollright Stones, where they meet Sharp and Blunt, an all-female morris dancing group from nearby Adderbury.

programme.desc: An account of Hitler's decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.desc: Adrian is put through the wringer when he attempts to apply for social benefits and Aveline reveals she has met a nice boy in London

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Freddie Boswell returns home, shocking Nellie with his new perspective on life. Julie and Aveline create chaos when they each announce they are leaving, while Adrian decides that Carmen is driven too much by pure lust rather than wanting to develop a proper relationship

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simon's journey begins at the Giant's Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A 20-year-old woman turns up on Lee's doorstep asking to borrow a cup of sugar. However, she has an ulterior motive - young Debbie believes Lee may be her father. After a period of denial, he remembers a one-night stand from a celebratory party thrown by Tim at the end of his accountancy exams in the 1980s. Sitcom, starring Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to sell a huge wheel of cheese which is long past its sell by date. Leroy gets sick of delivering goods by bicycle and demands his father invest in a van - and Gastric is on hand to sell them a suitable vehicle. Mrs Featherstone makes some frightening suggestions to Mr Newbold about taking their relationship further

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Tim Vine. Tim arrives home after a night out clubbing and realises he has picked up the wrong coat, one which contains a pocket full of drugs. After discovering the owner is Larry 'the Butcher' Stubbs, he enlists Lee's help to return the jacket without ending up in serious danger

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy commits a minor indiscretion while drunk, but claims to have no memory of the event. Determined to get to the bottom of the affair, she and Lee embark on an ambitious quest to discover the truth about whether it is possible to retain their memories of a heavy night of drinking - making use of liberal amounts of home-made potato hooch. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Alma's Not Normal
programme.desc: Lin has got herself in quite the legal tangle, so Alma decides to channel Erin Brockovich to get her mum out of trouble and bags her a pro bono solicitor. After the court hearing, Alma is left questioning everything and unsure what to do next, Jim tells her to write down all her family stories and all her experiences and use them in her stand-up routines

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted finds Clive's classic car gathering dust, and comes up with a devious money-making scheme, in which he raffles it as a prize, but he switches it for an old banger before delivering it to the lucky winner. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane and David Griffin

programme.title: Don't Wait Up
programme.desc: Tom's mother joins him for drinks, but after consuming too much alcohol she is offered Toby's bed - leading to an embarrassing situation. Nigel Havers and Tony Britton star

programme.title: Avoidance
programme.desc: Jonathan's attempt to win back Claire backfires when he discovers her big secret. Dan and Courtney get a surprise at their baby scan - it's a boy. Comedy, with Romesh Ranganathan, Jessica Knappett and Aisling Bea

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary looks for a new flatmate following Dermot's departure, and along comes Tony - but he may not be the perfect choice he appears to be. Meanwhile, Anthea's requests for a pay rise are greeted with banishment to the stationery cupboard

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher and the rest of Slade's inmates take advantage of the relaxed atmosphere - and of Barrowclough - with Mackay away on holiday. Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: The local mayor becomes embroiled in a sex scandal, so Boycie decides to stand for election as his replacement - unaware Llewellyn is planning to do the same. programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Young Mr Grace celebrates his birthday, so the staff decide to organise a cabaret in his honour. Another chance to see the 1978 Christmas special of the comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.desc: Compo and Clegg gatecrash Foggy's trip to see a female friend in Wales. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio lament the lack of fresh vegetables available due to a shortage of 'good honest muck' and wonder what it takes to find a half-decent carrot. Whimsical comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Yvonne organises a petition to get rid of Mr Partridge, who has been antagonising the staff, but there's no sign of the offending puppeteer. Ted and Spike return to their chalet for the night and see what appears to be his body floating in the pool with a knife in his back. Comedy, starring Paul Shane and Diane Holland

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon acquire a boat, planning to use it for coastal patrols, but after setting sail they get lost in fog. When the men finally reach dry land, they believe they have crossed the Channel and landed behind enemy lines. Vintage comedy first shown in 1969, starring Arthur Lowe

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Young Mr Grace celebrates his birthday, so the staff decide to organise a cabaret in his honour. Another chance to see the 1978 Christmas special of the comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville meets a man who claims to have been repeatedly abducted by aliens, and tries to persuade him to use his stories to boost sales. Leroy gets involved in a love triangle when Beth turns up for their date with a friend, while a divorcee comes to the shop hoping to find something that will stop her from missing her husband. Guest starring David Jason's former Only Fools and Horses co-star Sue Holderness

programme.desc: Arkwright comes up with a cunning plan and invents a Saint's day so that Granville can check the pockets of a foreign visitor who he suspects of shoplifting

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: War Weapons Week is to be marked in Walmington-on-Sea by a parade of all auxiliary services, including air-raid wardens, fire brigade, nurses, the rescue squad and of course, the Home Guard. programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mr Newbold shuts himself in his house to avoid Mrs Featherstone. programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part one of two. The veteran broadcaster follows life inside two prisons holding some of America's most notorious female criminals - Indiana Women's Prison and Rockville Correctional Facility in the same US state. Among the inmates he meets in the first edition are Sarah Pender, who went on the run while serving 110 years for double murder, before being captured four months later, and Martha Search, who is looking forward to getting to grips with modern technology when her 20-year sentence comes to an end

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Bedford Hills Correctional Facility in New York State to meet Sheila Davalloo, who has been convicted for murdering Anna Lisa Raymundo. However, in a bizarre twist to the story, Sheila has not yet started her 50-year sentence. First she must finish the 25 years she was given for the attempted murder of her husband. Sheila has remained silent about the killing for 14 years, so Piers tries to get her to finally open up, while also looking into why she wanted both of her victims out of the way

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Many hands make light work as Amanda Holden and Alan Carr invite local friends Fabrizio, Maria Pia and Carlos over for a plastering party, but things soon get out of hand as a spillage nearly ruins their newly laid terrazzo floor. On a shopping trek in the coastal city of Trapani, Amanda seeks modern soft furnishings for the living room, while Alan hunts for antique knick-knacks in the old town. Both reunite to style the newly designed space and reveal their labour of love, aka Casa Alamanda, to their Sicilian family

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Homes
programme.desc: The Property Brothers renovate Josefina's LA home to help make it more accessible for her sister

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team help Scott Jones, a 17-year-old disabled athlete in Cheltenham, who is eager to become more self-sufficient at home, with the challenge including added side and rear extensions, a wet room, access to an adapted kitchen and a state-of-the-art gym studio. Scott won gold at the 2013 IPC world championships and aims to develop his talents in order to participate at the 2020 Paralympics in Tokyo

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Derby to meet Rachel and Andy Smith, whose middle child Isaac has a severe form of cerebral palsy. The couple must be available at all times because Isaac sleeps in a hospital bed in the living room, but this is making life difficult for his siblings as their home design is in conflict with what the family really need. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team arrives to help, backed up by an army of local tradespeople - with designer Julia Kendall making sure they do not stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and the team head to the Worcestershire village of Eckington to help 17-year-old Antonia Payne-Cheney, who has Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, a rare and debilitating condition that causes her joints to regularly dislocate and means she cannot eat normally and is fed through a tube. The ambitious build features a functional, sterile space for medical procedures in her bedroom, a specially adapted wet room and an accessible area where she can socialise with friends and family

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: This time, Take That's Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. Elsewhere, a Springer Spaniel all the way from Guernsey is brought in for spinal surgery, and a Caucasian Shepherd puppy called Bear requires two hip replacements

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Coco, Sapphire and the Cheese Chis
programme.desc: Pete helps rescue six Chihuahuas from the home of a hoarder and witnesses the emotional handover of a pregnant pug and her sister

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: The detective reflects on 1984 when the team are flown to the Hebridean islands to investigate the death of a man found in a block of ice

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over
programme.desc: The presenter meets 23-year-old Lily Phillips, a high-profile adult content creator who has sparked both fascination and outrage in equal measure

programme.title: Lies, Deceit and Betrayal
programme.desc: Discover the most audacious scams in recent history, as we look at criminals who went to great lengths to con their victims - and ruin lives in the process. In Bath, the McAlinden family are persuaded by their friend Jeff Arundell to invest in a stock that is guaranteed to be on the up, but Jeff has other intentions for their money

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part two of two. The veteran broadcaster meets more inmates inside two of Indiana's most dangerous women's prisons. Among them are Dawn Hopkins, who was sentenced to life for murdering her three-month-old son, and Sarah 'Cindy' White, who was incarcerated as a teenager and will spend the rest of her life behind bars. The journalist also hears the remarkable story of Linda Darby who, after escaping from jail in 1972, spent the next 35 years on the run - before the law finally caught up with her

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey helps the Viney family declutter their home after grandma Sue filled their house with her deceased mum's belongings and the contents of a recently closed gift shop. Sue's children are desperate for a clear out so they can come over and spend more time with her. The team remove everything from the house including 532 plastic carrier bags, 205 hair rollers, 30 umbrellas, 17 digital cameras and 443 pairs of shoes. While the family sort through what they want to keep, recycle, donate or sell, carpenter Rob installs a pulldown bed for extra space and Dilly reinstates the downstairs as a functioning living and dining space

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Canadian comedian, actor and writer, Katherine Ryan, for a frank and honest conversation about her career, relationships and the changing shape of her family. Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: When Terry Guest had a brain injury, he was left with severe disabilities, and the only care available for him was an old people's dementia care home, where he remained for several years. His sister Tracey offered help, but like Terry's home, her house was also unsuitable. Nick Knowles's regulars and a team of South Yorkshire volunteers and suppliers step in to adapt Tracey's home and build Terry his own separate pad at the end of the garden

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Bidford-on-Avon, Warwickshire, to meet Chloe, who has a rare genetic disorder that means she has to stay in the living room of her family's house under constant care. The DIY SOS team are joined by Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and hundreds of Warwickshire volunteers to redesign the entire house to provide Chloe with independence, creating a self-contained apartment complete with bedroom, wet room and sitting room

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital
programme.desc: The DIY SOS team are out of their comfort zone this week, joining forces with two world-famous institutions - Great Ormond Street Hospital and the Chelsea Flower Show - for a special challenge. Gardener Chris Beardshaw has promised his gold medal-winning garden from the flower show to the hospital - now all Nick Knowles and his fellow builders have to do is crane the display across London and rebuild it on the hospital. It's a hugely ambitious build, with tricky logistics and emotional volunteers. But knowing how much the garden will prove welcome respite for families, the team is determined not to fail

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: In the first of a new series, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat a giant Newfoundland puppy with a painful knee and shoulder. What appears straightforward quickly turns into a much bigger concern, and Noel and his team are challenged to invent not one but two new implants for her. Meanwhile, a rescue black Labrador called Moogi is in need of treatment for severe elbow arthritis, and a golden Labrador born with a deformed front paw is offered a bionic solution in the form of a unique, custom-made prosthesis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Nick Knowles's regulars and a team of South Yorkshire volunteers and suppliers step in to adapt Tracey's home and build Terry his own separate pad at the end of the garden

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.sub-title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Cash-strapped Alison and Mike think all their problems have been solved when they inherit a grand country house, unaware that it is falling apart and teeming with the ghosts of former inhabitants. The spooks must act immediately to halt the new owners' plans to turn the house into a hotel, but their attempts to scare them away fail miserably. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe, Mathew Baynton, Simon Farnaby, Martha Howe-Douglas, Jim Howick, Laurence Rickard and Ben Willbond

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison finds herself hounded by the ghosts, who now realise she can both see and hear them, and tries to ignore their existence - a tactic which only exacerbates the problem when the Captain puts the ghosts onto a war footing. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the house into a home leads to a DIY disaster, saddling Lady Button with an ill-suited roommate, and Julian learns about his questionable legacy when the spooks discover the internet. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Lies, Deceit and Betrayal
programme.desc: The extraordinary case of Ian Paterson, a breast surgeon who subjected more than 1,000 of his patients to needless and damaging surgery

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Four years after the Daniels family moved into their home there are still moving boxes left unopened in the garage leaving little room for the family of five. Stacey, with the help of organiser Dilly, carpenter Rob and cleaner Iwan, puts all their belongings on display in a warehouse for them to see just how much unnecessary clutter they have including 492 DVDs, 35 happy birthday banners, 12 unopened chandeliers and 905 items of baby clothing

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dad's pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her family's arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Homes
programme.desc: The Scott brothers renovate a beachside home in Marina del Ray for owners Tammy and Dimiter

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, his team and a group of volunteers are in Monmouth, Wales, to transform the family home of Charlotte, a mother recovering from a stroke that left her with brain damage, speech loss and partial paralysis. The current layout of her home makes it difficult for Charlotte to move around and have her physiotherapy treatment, so the team redesign the house to make things easier for her, her husband Rob and their children

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part two of two. The veteran broadcaster meets more inmates inside two of Indiana's most dangerous women's prisons. Among them are Dawn Hopkins, who was sentenced to life for murdering her three-month-old son, and Sarah 'Cindy' White, who was incarcerated as a teenager and will spend the rest of her life behind bars. The journalist also hears the remarkable story of Linda Darby who, after escaping from jail in 1972, spent the next 35 years on the run - before the law finally caught up with her

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Many hands make light work as Amanda Holden and Alan Carr invite local friends Fabrizio, Maria Pia and Carlos over for a plastering party, but things soon get out of hand as a spillage nearly ruins their newly laid terrazzo floor. On a shopping trek in the coastal city of Trapani, Amanda seeks modern soft furnishings for the living room, while Alan hunts for antique knick-knacks in the old town. Both reunite to style the newly designed space and reveal their labour of love, aka Casa Alamanda, to their Sicilian family

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Homes
programme.desc: The Property Brothers renovate Josefina's LA home to help make it more accessible for her sister

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team help Scott Jones, a 17-year-old disabled athlete in Cheltenham, who is eager to become more self-sufficient at home, with the challenge including added side and rear extensions, a wet room, access to an adapted kitchen and a state-of-the-art gym studio. Scott won gold at the 2013 IPC world championships and aims to develop his talents in order to participate at the 2020 Paralympics in Tokyo

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Derby to meet Rachel and Andy Smith, whose middle child Isaac has a severe form of cerebral palsy. The couple must be available at all times because Isaac sleeps in a hospital bed in the living room, but this is making life difficult for his siblings as their home design is in conflict with what the family really need. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team arrives to help, backed up by an army of local tradespeople - with designer Julia Kendall making sure they do not stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and the team head to the Worcestershire village of Eckington to help 17-year-old Antonia Payne-Cheney, who has Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, a rare and debilitating condition that causes her joints to regularly dislocate and means she cannot eat normally and is fed through a tube. The ambitious build features a functional, sterile space for medical procedures in her bedroom, a specially adapted wet room and an accessible area where she can socialise with friends and family

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: This time, Take That's Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. Elsewhere, a Springer Spaniel all the way from Guernsey is brought in for spinal surgery, and a Caucasian Shepherd puppy called Bear requires two hip replacements

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team help Scott Jones, a 17-year-old disabled athlete in Cheltenham, who is eager to become more self-sufficient at home, with the challenge including added side and rear extensions, a wet room, access to an adapted kitchen and a state-of-the-art gym studio. programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Coco, Sapphire and the Cheese Chis
programme.desc: Pete helps rescue six Chihuahuas from the home of a hoarder and witnesses the emotional handover of a pregnant pug and her sister

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: The detective reflects on 1984 when the team are flown to the Hebridean islands to investigate the death of a man found in a block of ice

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over
programme.desc: The presenter meets 23-year-old Lily Phillips, a high-profile adult content creator who has sparked both fascination and outrage in equal measure

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part one of two. The veteran broadcaster follows life inside two prisons holding some of America's most notorious female criminals - Indiana Women's Prison and Rockville Correctional Facility in the same US state. Among the inmates he meets in the first edition are Sarah Pender, who went on the run while serving 110 years for double murder, before being captured four months later, and Martha Search, who is looking forward to getting to grips with modern technology when her 20-year sentence comes to an end

programme.title: Lies, Deceit and Betrayal
programme.desc: The extraordinary case of Ian Paterson, a breast surgeon who subjected more than 1,000 of his patients to needless and damaging surgery

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey helps the Viney family declutter their home after grandma Sue filled their house with her deceased mum's belongings and the contents of a recently closed gift shop. Sue's children are desperate for a clear out so they can come over and spend more time with her. The team remove everything from the house including 532 plastic carrier bags, 205 hair rollers, 30 umbrellas, 17 digital cameras and 443 pairs of shoes. While the family sort through what they want to keep, recycle, donate or sell, carpenter Rob installs a pulldown bed for extra space and Dilly reinstates the downstairs as a functioning living and dining space

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Canadian comedian, actor and writer, Katherine Ryan, for a frank and honest conversation about her career, relationships and the changing shape of her family. Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Bidford-on-Avon, Warwickshire, to meet Chloe, who has a rare genetic disorder that means she has to stay in the living room of her family's house under constant care. The DIY SOS team are joined by Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and hundreds of Warwickshire volunteers to redesign the entire house to provide Chloe with independence, creating a self-contained apartment complete with bedroom, wet room and sitting room

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital
programme.desc: The DIY SOS team are out of their comfort zone this week, joining forces with two world-famous institutions - Great Ormond Street Hospital and the Chelsea Flower Show - for a special challenge. Gardener Chris Beardshaw has promised his gold medal-winning garden from the flower show to the hospital - now all Nick Knowles and his fellow builders have to do is crane the display across London and rebuild it on the hospital. It's a hugely ambitious build, with tricky logistics and emotional volunteers. But knowing how much the garden will prove welcome respite for families, the team is determined not to fail

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: In the first of a new series, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat a giant Newfoundland puppy with a painful knee and shoulder. What appears straightforward quickly turns into a much bigger concern, and Noel and his team are challenged to invent not one but two new implants for her. Meanwhile, a rescue black Labrador called Moogi is in need of treatment for severe elbow arthritis, and a golden Labrador born with a deformed front paw is offered a bionic solution in the form of a unique, custom-made prosthesis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Lies, Deceit and Betrayal
programme.desc: Discover the most audacious scams in recent history, as we look at criminals who went to great lengths to con their victims - and ruin lives in the process. In Bath, the McAlinden family are persuaded by their friend Jeff Arundell to invest in a stock that is guaranteed to be on the up, but Jeff has other intentions for their money

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Pat's family make their annual pilgrimage to the site of his death and he begs Alison to pass on a message from the other side - a plan complicated when he realises a shocking truth about his marriage. Meanwhile, Mike struggles to come to terms with his bathroom-based paranoia, and Kitty tries to find out where babies come from. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey coaches the Smith family through a mammoth declutter as their possessions are laid out in a warehouse as though it were an art installation they discover 207 loose nappies, 145 Punjabi suits and 24 old toothbrushes amongst other things. Things get emotional as Raaj lets go of some of her late sister's belongings whom she lost to Covid. Carpenter Rob works hard to make their three-bedroom house accommodate six people and Dilly creates a brand-new larder while reorganising the kitchen

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
