programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to the Isle of Sheppey in Kent to meet two extraordinary dads, Garry and Kyle, who have adopted four vulnerable children - three of whom have special needs - to give them the best life possible. But their cramped three-bedroom bungalow is making family life difficult. So to give this family the space they so desperately need, the DIY SOS builders are undertaking their most ambitious project yet - to demolish the bungalow and build a bespoke five-bedroom house in its place. But with just nine days to complete the task, it looks like being their toughest one yet

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus has his eye on a classic Renault 5 that's cheap and clean. Will isn't sure but Gus twists his arm. It's super-rare, and has little wrong with it - apart from a cooling issue that they can't see, let alone fix. Series 2, Episode 7

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Northamptonshire in search of more upcycling prospects. Simon finds some night vision goggles and an antique snooker table that's seen better days, while Henry chooses an enormous US army truck as one of his picks

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Off the coast of Yorkshire Siwrengale is heading out for the beginning of the North Sea whelk season. Skipper Darren Warters is on the hunt with sons Regan and Lewis. But winter storms have badly damaged their pots - and miles of rope must be untangled before they can begin fishing in earnest. Reliance III is south east of Shetland, where skipper John Clarke is hunting for whiting, while Endeavour 5 is far off in the North Atlantic in search of squid - but when the prey proves elusive, the pressure starts to mount

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: Following a dedicated team of craftspeople as they work to complete a five-year, £25 million project to restore Canterbury Cathedral to its former glory

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: The team build a protection deck for the Edinburgh North Bridge, using 300km of scaffolding to encase it in an exoskeleton of constantly changing steel

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 1973 Ford Escort Mexico is the team's most expensive purchase to date. To save some cash Derek helps on their second buy - a 1968 Riley Kestrel

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business. Featured vehicles include a Mini Cooper and Chevrolet Pick-up truck

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Examining the use of forced labour to build weapons and vehicles for the German army, the downfall of the last Russian tsar, and the construction of the Hindenburg Line

programme.title: Churchill's Bid to Abolish Britain
programme.desc: The story of how Winston Churchill tried to save France and destroy Hitler by destroying Britain and creating the 'Franco-British Union' - a plan that was to crash and burn

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the former drawing offices of Harland and Wolff shipyard in Belfast, as the team examines items brought in by the public. Finds include an early Victorian medicine chest, a historic document marking the end of the Second World War, and a rare pair of Irish plate buckets, which turn out to be worth the price of a new car

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team set up at the Bishop's Palace in Wells, for the first of two programmes from the Somerset city. Items brought in for valuation include one of the show's earliest recorded objects. Plus, a plate that reputedly belonged to Captain Scott during his ill-fated expedition to the Antarctic

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A 1970 Excalibur Roadstar, once owned by Roy Orbison, arrives at Thornton-le-Dale and Derek heads to County Durham to collect a 1935 Austin 7 Ulster

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Two iconic classics find their way to the Mathewsons' auction room. Dave heads to north Manchester to pick up an Austin A30 and a Morris 8E that's had an alternative paint job

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Examining the use of forced labour to build weapons and vehicles for the German army, the downfall of the last Russian tsar, and the construction of the Hindenburg Line

programme.title: Churchill's Bid to Abolish Britain
programme.desc: The story of how Winston Churchill tried to save France and destroy Hitler by destroying Britain and creating the 'Franco-British Union' - a plan that was to crash and burn

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Animals that live in Asia's deserts and dry grasslands, including the Gobi bear, one of the rarest animals on Earth who communicate by leaving their scent on ancient trees. The Thar in India is the most densely populated desert in the world, and a place where Demoiselle cranes migrate thousands of miles to reach, being welcomed with grain put out by the local Jain community

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the work of people striving to protect Asia's endangered wildlife, and what routes conservation could take in the future. In Japan, scientists take pregnant sharks, killed accidentally in fishing nets, and rescue their unborn young in an incubating system that simulates a womb. The programme also visits a village that used to be a hotbed of poaching and is now a centre for bird tourism

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A fully restored Class 47 diesel engine is transported from its home at Crewe Heritage Centre in Cheshire to the Ecclesbourne Valley Railway in Derbyshire

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: The crew of Karen of Ladram hunts hake off Cornwall, but the trip is thrown into jeopardy when a crew member's health deteriorates and their catch is decimated when seals get to the fish first. Elsewhere, a rookie crew works in the Irish Sea, trainee skipper Michael steps up in a storm, and Tydus heads out in search of crabs

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europe's only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Car enthusiasts Gus and Will want to buy a VW Beetle. They locate a guy selling a project car, but fear it's almost too good to be true at less than £1,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn an old, unloved filing cabinet into a valuable and desirable storage unit and convert a Bakelite voltmeter into a retro Bluetooth speaker

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: The crew of the scalloper Golden Promise pushes Force 6 winds to reach newly opening scallops beds ahead of their rivals. With the boat first to the grounds, they are in for a big pay day - but only once they cover the £8,000 fuel expenses to get here. Family owned trawler Adenia hunts for 1,000 tons of mackerel to sell to the lucrative Asian sushi market, and skipper Ivan Toms and son Rhys fish Cornwall's inshore waters in Maxine's Pride, one of the oldest trawlers in UK waters. Can they catch enough to give themselves a wage and put money aside for repairs?

programme.title: Mammals

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough has a passion for birds' eggs, which protect the young from the outside world at the same time as allowing them to breathe and are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. In this documentary, the broadcaster reveals the secrets of birds' eggs from creation to hatching, exploring how they are made, the reasons behind their shape and their intrinsic purpose

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Off the coast of Yorkshire Siwrengale is heading out for the beginning of the North Sea whelk season. Skipper Darren Warters is on the hunt with sons Regan and Lewis. But winter storms have badly damaged their pots - and miles of rope must be untangled before they can begin fishing in earnest. Reliance III is south east of Shetland, where skipper John Clarke is hunting for whiting, while Endeavour 5 is far off in the North Atlantic in search of squid - but when the prey proves elusive, the pressure starts to mount

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Russia's remote Amur River, where the mysterious deaths of three men appear to be linked to a freshwater giant - a beast that can exceed 18ft in length and weigh more than a ton. However, he encounters a wall of silence and begins to suspect he has stumbled on to a dark secret that isn't supposed to be uncovered

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Ormerod opens a can of worms while attending to the problems of a local family, and when violence spills on to the wards he is caught in the crossfire. Meanwhile, Aisling's adoption is official, but Sister Brigid is confused by her feelings. Last-ever episode of the hospital drama, starring Robert Daws, Linda Armstrong, Amy Robbins and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Early morning chat show presented by Lorraine Kelly, with celebrity interviews, entertainment news, cooking tips, fashion features and musical guests.

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Tommy Martin interviews the former Republic of Ireland footballer, who reflects on a career that began in the League of Ireland, and saw him move to the top of the English game

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: In this edition, Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from York, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the big finale game

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: The comedian and dog-lover meets some of the people who dedicate their lives to helping sick and abandoned canines in Delhi, where 400,000 pooches live on the streets. First up are a street pup with a potentially fatal virus and a golden retriever that will not stop chewing its paw. Paul also rolls up his sleeves like never before as he rescues a litter of puppies, born in the grounds of Lodhi Gardens, from almost certain death

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade visits Papua New Guinea, an island renowned for cannibalism, macabre rituals, headhunters and bitter tribal conflict. However, a new threat is sweeping through the region and he sets out on the trail of a creature that has reportedly been tearing chunks out of fishermen and devouring male body parts

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Shaun, Gina, John and Jane

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Dom is hoping to be impressed by the style choices of three potential dates

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Hope, Angel and Serena all hope to impress registrar Dominic with their style choices

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines an incident in 2003 where two girls were left fighting for their lives, having been caught in an exchange of gunfire between two rival gangs. The case had far-reaching implications, putting gun violence in Britain's inner cities at the top of the political agenda and changing how the courts protect key witnesses

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The next generation of engineering wonders, beginning with an international team in Norway who are building the largest polar expedition vessel in the world

programme.title: Unbeatable

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former Crystal Palace and Republic of Ireland defender, who discusses the highs and lows of his career

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: The comedian helps more of the sick and abandoned dogs in Delhi. He meets a pup whose ears are infected and tries to train a boisterous labrador that cannot be rehomed because of its behavioural problems. He also goes out and about helping to treat some of the 400,000 waifs and strays on the city's streets

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: Paul helps a traumatised dog that desperately needs to find a home, and joins Delhi's vets once more as they go out and about to treat injured street dogs. However, the pooches are not the only ones needing medical attention when the presenter falls ill

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade visits New Zealand in search of a mysterious 8ft creature as described more than 200 years ago by a famous explorer. In trying to discover whether he is dealing simply with a legend or a real flesh-and-blood monster that has allegedly attacked divers and even seized a child, he finds the remote rivers and deep lakes would provide an ideal refuge for the predator

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Chris, Trevor, Fiona and Susie

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Josh, Scott and Tim go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges hoping to win the affections of Sarah from Lancashire.

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Suitors George, Justin and Lewis compete for the affections of Londoner Jen. Which of the three single shoppers' fashion choices will win them a date?

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Robert Rinder examines the case of Claire Throssell. After years in a controlling marriage, she managed to leave, taking her two young sons with her. Life would have been perfect, if it wasn't for Claire's ex-husband Darren Sykes still having access to their children. During one fatal unsupervised visit, Claire's life would be destroyed

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The race to build a massive new rollercoaster in Canada's biggest theme park before it opens its gates for the summer - a ride with a record-breaking 90-degree vertical drop

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Limitless Win
programme.desc: Engaged couple Zanesh and Melike continue their game, hoping they can cash out with enough money to put together an unforgettable Turkish-Indian wedding. Ant and Dec then welcome father-and-son team David and Mark, who take on the never-ending money ladder

programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Another edition of Unseen Bits that is bursting at the seams with exclusive unseen action from the Villa alongside fun, flirting, and fallouts.

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer talks about his life inside and outside the ring. From growing up as a triplet to when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic Gold medal in boxing in 1992

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: The comedian and dog-lover presents his final report from Delhi, where 400,000 pooches live on the streets. Paul joins in with the celebrations for Diwali, the Hindu festival of light, with the people and animals of one of Delhi's oldest dog shelters. He also helps a traumatised puppy make new friends, joins medics as they rush to rescue a four-week-old street pup who may have been hit by a car and falls in love with an elderly black labrador who lives at the rescue centre. Will he be able to resist bringing the pooch back to Britain with him?

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the jockey, who reflects on a pioneering career, a life immersed in horse racing, and her role as a role model to other women

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: It's the day of Saif's funeral, and as Jenn watches closely for any suspicious behaviour, divisions within the Rahman household widen. Adnan is anxious about the repercussions of last night's incident, but Jenn's attempts to help are overshadowed by Mariam's reaction to events at the funeral. Back at the station, Clarkie's investigation leads to a significant breakthrough about a possible motive for Saif's murder, which suggests the killer might be connected to the Rahmans. But before Jenn can investigate further, some shocking personal news throws her off course. Crime drama, starring Marsha Thomason

programme.title: The Big Interview
programme.desc: Thirty-nine-year-old Sophie Toscan du Plantier was killed outside her holiday home near Toormore, West Cork in December 1996. Now, years on from what became a murder case, Colette Fitzpatrick sits down with suspect Ian Bailey to discuss events related to the incident. Bailey was never charged with the crime in Ireland

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Herman's behaviour becomes increasingly erratic and he is sent on compulsory leave. Will a private inspection of Kanit House bring him, Angela and their colleagues the major breakthrough they crave? Charles, meanwhile, is in Karachi attempting to set up business in Europe and as he attempts to snare a buyer for his gems, he faces up to memories of his past, while Marie-Andree and Ajay vie for ascendancy within the group

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi comes to the aid of John and Ilona, who bought the 10-bedroom Yeoldon House Hotel in Bideford, Devon as a retirement project. Despite having no industry experience, they ploughed their savings into the project. However, the reality has been far from the good life, with out-of-season occupancy below 10 per cent and the pair unable to afford a salary

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: The net begins to close on Charles and Marie-Andree in India,and he hatches a daring plan to escape to South America, but he is becoming reckless and soon everything spirals out of control. In Bangkok, Herman, Angela and the Action Committee face up to life after the Sobhraj case

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Litvinenko
programme.desc: In November 2006, DI Brent Hyatt and DS Jim Dawson are summoned to University College Hospital in London to interview Alexander Litvinenko, a former Russian spy who claims to have been poisoned. His wife Marina tells the detectives that doctors are sceptical of his story, though his deteriorating physical condition is clear for everyone to see. Litvinenko provides a meticulous account of who he believes to have killed him, and also the man responsible for ordering his murder, none other than Vladimir Putin. Fact-based drama, starring David Tennant

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: In the aftermath of a brutal rescue mission, Amy is convinced that what she's believed up until now has been a lie, but all the evidence points the other way. With Amy completely isolated, Kirsten begs her to come home, but she cannot let go of her suspicions. Despite huge pressure from above, Amy refuses to leave Wudyan, putting herself in grave danger

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe risks his career to help Dawson when she is accused of theft following a raid on local crook Frank Batley's home and £400 goes missing. Bernie faces a tough decision when he learns he has a daughter he never knew existed. Starring Joe McFadden and Peter Benson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke help Claire and Richard in Barnsley. They are desperate for somewhere to socialise and reconnect with friends and family, following difficult fertility treatment. After a day of hard work, hopefully their revamped garden will work wonders

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode prepares jerk chicken with rice and peas, while Lisa Faulkner makes a vegetarian curry full of paneer and potatoes

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: The presenter, along with interior designer John Amabile, transforms the property of foster carer Wendy White in Greenwich, south-east London. They tackle her lounge and bedroom in the hope of turning the house into a tranquil and relaxing home

programme.title: Alex Polizzi's Secret Italy
programme.desc: For the final leg of her journey, the hotelier heads to the south, a region she knows little about. In Puglia, she marvels at the local beehive-shaped dry stone huts and samples a wine with a difference, before heading to the Monte Pollino national park. Alex concludes her adventure in the city of Matera, where she attends the annual Festa della Madonna Bruna, a week-long carnival culminating in a pagan-style rite of destruction and a firework display

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ruby is attempting to win Blaketons heart, and Nick's colleagues are enjoying teasing him over his mum's potential romance with the sergeant. An illiterate man, Eddie, is outraged when a cottage to rent was promised to him by his aunt, but instead ends up being sold. His girlfriend calls off their wedding because they don't have a house! There is also an investigation of a peeping Tom in the village. Auntie Eileen comes to visit, and Ruby sees her chance to return to London

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A fully relocatable polar research station in Antarctica, which was inspired by a revolutionary icebreaking ship and ingenious designs for pre-fabricated buildings

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits Horley near Gatwick to help Quasim, the owner, improve his ailing Surrey guesthouse. With 13 other B&Bs all battling for the same trade, he is struggling to make the monthly repayments. Alex can see the potential in Quasim's business, but curbing his expensive tastes to maximise profits turns out to be far from easy

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain pretends he is in pain to avoid being discharged. Todd and Vanessa arrange a date, while Bear's anxiety causes him to snap at Mandy and Kerry

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tim attempts to get through to Will, Todd's attitude attracts suspicion, Daniel meets an intruder entering his flat, and Hope's teasing causes Sam to snap

programme.title: The Royal
programme.sub-title: Should I Stay or Should I Go Now
programme.desc: Timid new student nurse Faye Clark is faced with a trying first day when a model collapses after taking magic mushrooms. Lizzie falls for an injured motorcycle stuntman who is convinced he is destined to live fast and die young due to a hereditary condition - but Ormerod comes up with a different diagnosis. Lauren Drummond, Michelle Hardwick, James Daffern and Robert Daws star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans returns as the young Inspector Morse in the first of four feature-length episodes. A baffling suicide, an anguished father searching for his missing daughter and the smash-and-grab robbery of medieval artefacts throw the detective in at the deep end as he resumes his role with Oxford City Police following a four-month absence from duty. As the locals go to the polls in a closely fought by-election and a beauty contest builds to its conclusion, Endeavour must navigate the choppy waters of both worlds as his investigation shakes the highest pillars of Oxford society. Co-starring Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw, Jack Laskey and Sean Rigby

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Buried resentments rise to the surface when a German arrives in Aidensfield, and an elderly couple have a wedding anniversary to remember

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A booby-trapped cuckoo clock kills a guest at an engagement party, sending the detectives through a maze of family secrets. Detective drama set in a picturesque rural county where death lurks around every corner. Mystery drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, with Nick Hendrix, Annette Badland and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tim confronts Trisha with his revised view of their relationship, the police begin to gather hard evidence, Sam encounters a threat in the ginnel, and Ben is relieved when Will decides to remain at the Rovers

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Todd blackmails Charity, while Sarah heads to hospital to confront Cain about his self-pity and Bob panics when he discovers he is a player short for the men's darts team

programme.title: The Suspect
programme.desc: The rift between Joe and his wife Julianne sees him at his lowest ebb, but he is still determined to untangle the web he is caught up in. He believes that there's a pattern to be found amongst all the coincidences and a breakthrough sets him on a new path of investigation. Meanwhile, DI Ruiz and DS Devi are newly determined to get justice for Catherine, but their own enquiries have hit a roadblock

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: A sinister inquiry into skeletal remains found in a Brighton storm drain takes Grace down a labyrinthine trail through the past, but it's not only on the professional front that he is chasing ghosts.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rivalry between two brass bands gets out of hand and a musician dies of suspected poisoning. Dawn is swept off her feet by a handsome stranger, leaving Gina less than happy when she announces she is moving to Paris with him. Joe and Rachel succumb to their attraction, only for Carol to catch them kissing. Rural drama, guest starring Keith Barron and Peter Wight

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Western Star
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke have just one day to transform a grey and windswept roof terrace in Basingstoke, Hampshire into a cosy and colourful outdoor room for owner Aimee

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have recipes for summertime centrepieces for entertaining guests, including slow-cooked beef rendang, courgette and pea fritters, prawns served in a bread bowl, a ginger mocktail, and ricotta doughnuts with chocolate sauce

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Attention! Pete and the team are on makeover manoeuvres today transforming a mobile home for ex-serviceman Jim Skasick. Designer Ben Hillman takes inspiration from Jim's American roots to bring a ranch theme to the lounge / diner and military theme to the bedroom.

programme.title: Alex Polizzi's Secret Italy
programme.desc: Alex embraces her lifelong love of Italy and travels North to South, taking in the breath-taking cities of Venice, Milan, Rome and the Amalfi Coast, before unearthing hidden tourist gems in Puglia. She begins in Sardinia

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Buried resentments rise to the surface when a German arrives in Aidensfield, and an elderly couple have a wedding anniversary to remember

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction and maintenance of the International Space Station, revealing the influence of an unconventional aircraft designer and a 19th-century rebreather

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi is called to the Gipsy Hill Hotel in Exeter, where owner Grace has plans to retire and hand the business to her daughter Jackie, who currently manages the hotel. But profits are falling and Grace worries there will be no business to pass down. Alex immediately spots that the hotel is stuck in the past, with its old-fashioned decor. Can she convince the owner to release her grip on the purse strings and allow her daughter to take ownership now?

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Todd blackmails Charity, while Sarah heads to hospital to confront Cain about his self-pity and Bob panics when he discovers he is a player short for the men's darts team

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tim confronts Trisha with his revised view of their relationship, the police begin to gather hard evidence, Sam encounters a threat in the ginnel, and Ben is relieved when Will decides to remain at the Rovers

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A cancer patient dies unexpectedly after a visit from Dr Ormerod and the grieving family points the finger at him - leading to his arrest on suspicion of murder. Dr Ellis holds a clinic at the prison and becomes trapped when a riot breaks out, while Faye has to overcome her fear of needles to give Jack a tetanus shot. Hospital drama, starring Robert Daws, Neil McDermott, Lauren Drummond and Gareth Hale

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A woman with Alzheimer's disease inadvertently commits a series of crimes, while Gina becomes suspicious of two customers staying at the pub

programme.title: The Au Pair
programme.desc: After a chaotic day, seamstress Zoe agrees to her husband's suggestion of bringing in an au pair to help with her elderly father and the step-children. Enter Sandrine, who is capable and composed - but as the newcomer settles in, subtle shifts in the family dynamic begin to unfold, setting off a chain of events that puts Zoe in danger. Drama, starring David Suchet and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A phone call from Melanie elicits a revelation for Ben, Tim shares an update with Will, Summer eyes a fresh start in America, and Sam's grip on reality starts to slip

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity struggles to find the money to ensure Todd keeps her secret, while Cain wants revenge when Moira admits Robert planted the identity documents that got her arrested

programme.title: Safe Harbour
programme.desc: The Walsh family business is in jeopardy of becoming over-crowed when a new player comes to town. Crime thriller, starring Alfie Allen, Jack Gleeson and Charlie Murphy

programme.title: Party Animals
programme.desc: Scott takes time off from work in the wake of Jake's death and is happy to receive a surprise visit from Ashika, but a jealous Sophie tells her rival he is only using her to gain Conservative contacts. Elsewhere, George Morgan receives a public dressing-down about exaggerated tales concerning his upbringing - much to James's delight. However, the old-school Tory knows who is to blame and already has revenge in mind. Political drama, starring Andrew Buchan and Shelley Conn

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: A body is dredged up from the English Channel, setting in motion a truly disturbing investigation that forces Detective Superintendent Roy Grace to ask himself just how far someone might go to save the ones they love. Crime drama based on the books by Peter James, starring John Simm, with Richie Campbell, Rakie Ayola, Laura Elphinstone, Brad Morrison, Craig Parkinson and Zoe Tapper

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason is called to help Evelyn, a widow who is receiving threats from a corrupt associate of her late husband. Her son-in-law Alan is reluctant to sever his ties with the crook, knowing he provides vital revenue - which forces her into silence. But the police urge Evelyn to cooperate after her car is run off the road and her grandson goes missing. Meanwhile, a Polish visitor arrives in search of a long-lost love

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke head to Northamptonshire to help create a low-allergen garden for a couple whose young son is affected by severe allergies

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner return with the culinary show, beginning with demonstrations of how to cook the perfect versions of some of the nation's favourite meals, including steak and chips and a chocolate cake. There is also a simple chicken pie and twice-baked goat's cheese souffles

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Single father and keen gardener Rob receives a surprise home makeover courtesy of designer Leah Hughes and and her support team. The lounge and bedroom of Rob's Surrey home are given a new lease of life after suffering years of wear and tear during the raising of four children

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: Between 1975 and 1980, serial killer Peter Sutcliffe - known in the press as the Yorkshire Ripper - murdered 13 women and attacked at least seven more in the north of England. In this programme, psychologists and criminology experts analyse the personality of Sutcliffe and look at events in his childhood and adult life that may have triggered his descent into murder, and also examine how he managed to avoid suspicion despite being interviewed by police numerous times

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: Between May 2005 and July 2006, Samuel Dieteman and brothers Dale and Jeff Hausner terrorised the residents of Phoenix, Arizona, as they turned drive-by shootings into sport, keeping score as they picked off their victims at random. This film delves into their backgrounds and talks to those who knew them in an effort to find out what turned them into killers, while detectives, journalists and criminal psychologists also offer an insight into their motives

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: The Salvage Squad
programme.desc: Brian Dowling and his fashion-forward design team are on a mission to prove to the nation that you can achieve the home renovation of your dreams without it literally costing the earth. Changing Rooms meets the Repair Shop with a new sustainable twist, the challenge here is to transform our bland old rooms, furniture and fittings into unique and stunning show stoppers, using only upcycling and sustainable methods. This is the home makeover format for the world we live in today, as 2 households per week, each with a team consisting of an interior designer and an up-cycling wiz, have just four days and 1,000 euro to renovate and rejuvenate a tired spaces in their home, filling them with home-made, re-used and revamped furnishings. Two families are selected from the same region each week. Each family has a tired room in need of renovation and re-imagining containing a potential treasure trove of up-cycling gems
